scenario: You see a woman spanking her child with a spatula for getting bad grades in school.
moral_expl: Physical punishment for academic performance is widely viewed as excessive and harmful, undermining child welfare and effective discipline.

scenario: I was supposed to go see my father before he died. He was ill and my mother was supposed to come into town so we could see him. She didn't come, so we never made it before he passed away.
moral_expl: Failing to visit a dying parent is regrettable and may reflect poorly on filial responsibility, but the failure was due to circumstances beyond full control (mother not coming), mitigating moral blame.

scenario: Person X stepped in when a friend at a pub was getting assaulted for being dark skinned.
moral_expl: Intervening to protect someone from racial assault demonstrates courage, empathy, and a commitment to justice and human dignity.

scenario: Gave a beggar a five-dollar bill at Christmas time
moral_expl: Giving to those in need is good, but impact is limited and context matters.

scenario: You are the safety officer in charge of a fun park. One of the metal arms of the ferris wheel suddenly breaks because of a structural defect. Four people are stranded in a cabin 80 metres up in the air. Another person is in a cabin just a few metres from ground level and is able to get off alone. The whole structure is falling down.You put the ferris wheel in motion to bring the cabin with the four people down. You know that the person who is getting off now will go up again and will die as the ferris wheel collapses, but the other four will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save four involves difficult ethical trade-offs; though intended to minimize loss, deliberately causing someone's death raises serious moral concerns.

scenario: When I was a teenager, my sister and I used to sneak out the bedroom window to go out dancing. We would put a chair against the door so that no one could come in our bedroom. A couple of times we got caught.
moral_expl: Sneaking out as a teenager involves disobedience and risk, but is often seen as a common rite of passage; moral concern is low given context and age.

scenario: Person X ordered pizza while at their friend's farewell party.
moral_expl: Ordering pizza at a farewell party is a neutral to slightly positive act, depending on context—could be seen as contributing to the gathering or as inconsiderate if inappropriate. Generally not morally significant.

scenario: It is evening. A friend calls you saying that he has a terrible migraine headache. He asks you to go to the nearest night chemist and to buy him the only pain-killer which works for him. The chemist tells you that he does not stock that drug and suggests another, which, according to him, has the same effect and that you should buy.You buy the migraine drug that the chemist suggests. You make your friend take it without showing him the box. He will think he is taking the one he asked for.
moral_expl: Deceiving a friend, even with good intentions, violates trust and autonomy. While the motive is compassionate, withholding the truth about the medication undermines informed consent and could have unintended health consequences.

scenario: Took a friend out for a beer to celebrate his promotion
moral_expl: Celebrating a friend's success with a beer is a kind and supportive gesture, reflecting goodwill and camaraderie. While alcohol consumption carries minor ethical considerations for some, the act of honoring a friend's achievement is generally positive.

scenario: Fires teargas into a crowd of civilians.
moral_expl: Using teargas on civilians violates their rights, causes unnecessary harm, and undermines public safety and trust, especially without justification or proportionality.

scenario: Complimented coworkers on their clothing and appearance
moral_expl: Complimenting coworkers can be kind and affirming, but must be respectful and appropriate to avoid discomfort or perceived impropriety.

scenario: I was in a relationship with my best friend's cousin. She was really beautiful, but as time went on I became less attracted to her. I tried to tell her I didn't really want to see her anymore and she flipped out.
moral_expl: Ending a relationship due to diminished attraction is understandable, but the moral acceptability depends on how it was communicated; the 'flipped out' reaction suggests emotional distress, though the act itself isn't highly immoral.

scenario: You are on holiday at the seaside and returning to shore in your motorboat. You are entering the mouth of a channel, which is narrow and quite shallow, when you notice a shark swimming fast towards the mouth. At the end of the channel is a swimmer, who is calmly bathing. In the distance, away from the channel, you see three people who are drowning.You turn quickly out of the channel, leaving the mouth free. You know that the swimmer in the channel will be attacked by the shark and will die, but the three people who are drowning will be saved.
moral_expl: Choosing to save three over one involves a utilitarian trade-off, though deliberately allowing harm to the swimmer raises ethical concerns about duty and foreseen consequences.

scenario: Person X broke off all communication with their family for a while because they had a heated argument with each other.
moral_expl: Temporary communication break seen as understandable but not ideal for resolving family conflicts.

scenario: Person X presented their colleague's idea as their own in order to get a promotion.
moral_expl: Stealing credit undermines trust, fairness, and integrity, harming workplace relationships and ethical standards.

scenario: You see an employee joking with competitors about how bad his company did last year.
moral_expl: Joking with competitors about one's company's poor performance shows disloyalty and undermines trust, though intent and context may moderate severity.

scenario: Someone posts on social media bragging about making out with multiple strangers the night before
moral_expl: While personal behavior is a matter of choice, public bragging about intimate acts may be seen as lacking discretion or respect for others' privacy and boundaries, though it doesn't directly harm others.

scenario: You see a girl shooting geese repeatedly with a pellet gun out in the woods.
moral_expl: Harming animals for no reason is considered cruel and unethical by most people.

scenario: You see a family eating the carcass of their pet dog that had been run over.
moral_expl: Cultural norms strongly oppose consuming pets, which are seen as family members; however, context such as survival or cultural practice may influence judgment, though it remains deeply unsettling to most.

scenario: Person X stole the collection tin of a blind beggar on the street.
moral_expl: Stealing from a vulnerable, blind individual is a severe violation of trust and compassion, exploiting those in greatest need.

scenario: Refused to hold the door for a man in a wheelchair
moral_expl: Refusing to assist someone with a disability, especially when a simple act like holding a door could aid their access, shows disregard for basic courtesy and inclusivity.

scenario: Spent time voluntarily tutoring disadvantaged students
moral_expl: Voluntarily tutoring disadvantaged students demonstrates compassion, promotes equity, and contributes positively to society without expectation of reward.

scenario: Person X saw a homeless person in the rain, so they gave the person their jacket and umbrella, plus $20 for a hot meal
moral_expl: Helping a homeless person in distress demonstrates compassion, empathy, and generosity, especially with personal sacrifice involved.

scenario: For a while I was a security officer at a bank. One day I was walking by the front door when I saw a fifty-dollar bill. I was tempted to take the money but instead I reported it to the manager.
moral_expl: Resisting temptation and reporting found money upholds honesty and professional integrity, especially important for a security officer.

scenario: Heckled a woman speaking on human rights
moral_expl: Heckling someone, especially during a human rights speech, disrupts dialogue, disrespects free expression, and undermines important discourse on justice and dignity.

scenario: Criticized an old woman for being too slow
moral_expl: Criticizing someone for being slow, especially an elderly person, shows disrespect and lack of empathy, promoting ageism and unkindness.

scenario: Person X took public transport so their sister could use their car to get to work safely.
moral_expl: Sacrificing personal convenience to ensure a family member's safety and reliability is considerate and supportive.

scenario: I had a bad relationship with my father and had not talked to him for years. He had left my mother. When my mother died I gathered the strength to call him and tell him that she died and that I loved him.
moral_expl: Reaching out to a estranged parent during a time of loss demonstrates compassion, forgiveness, and emotional courage, especially after a difficult history.

scenario: My husband ran a business and I worked in his shop. Once a rather unsavory character came in and handed my husband something. So, I looked through my husband's wallet to see what the man gave him.
moral_expl: Invasion of privacy justified by concern for husband's safety and business integrity.

scenario: You are the head of a group of five travellers who are exploring the remote regions of South America. During the trip, you inadvertently camp in the sacred cemetery of a local tribe. The members of the tribe capture you and condemn you all to death. After some negotiation, they propose a possible alternative in the form of a human sacrifice that will pacify their god who has been offended.You accept the tribe’s proposal, and during the ceremony, you kill one of the travellers in the group. This person will die, but you and the other four travellers’ lives will be spared.
moral_expl: Sacrificing an innocent person to save others under duress involves profound moral conflict; while it may preserve more lives, it violates individual rights and ethical principles against killing. Cultural context and coercion complicate judgment, but taking a life in this manner is generally seen as ethically unjustifiable.

scenario: Rescued an injured kitten from a tree
moral_expl: Rescuing an injured kitten demonstrates compassion and care for vulnerable life, widely viewed as morally positive.

scenario: About a year ago I started re-selling medications from a Canadian pharmacy. I helped a great deal of elderly people so that they didn't have to pay exorbitant amounts for their medications that they needed.
moral_expl: Helping elderly access affordable medications is commendable and addresses a serious social issue, but re-selling prescription drugs without proper authorization violates laws and medical safety protocols, risking patient health and undermining regulatory systems. The moral benefit of altruistic intent is offset by legal and safety concerns.

scenario: I was at the pharmacy buying something and I noticed a man who was sitting outside selling trinkets. He was homeless and it was freezing out. So I went next door to a store and bought him some food and new clothes.
moral_expl: Helping a homeless person in need, especially in harsh weather, demonstrates compassion and generosity, though long-term solutions may be more effective.

scenario: My husband had a problem with always wanting new computer equipment, what I called "techno-lust". It was a really huge financial problem, and I told him he had to go on a 5-year plan to financially police himself or I would divorce him. He has greatly improved.
moral_expl: Setting boundaries to address financial responsibility in a relationship is reasonable, and encouraging self-discipline promotes long-term stability. The ultimatum, while firm, serves a constructive purpose and led to positive change.

scenario: Took the neighbor's newspaper
moral_expl: Taking someone else's property without permission violates trust and respect for ownership, even if the item is minor.

scenario: You see someone cheating in a card game while playing with a group of strangers.
moral_expl: Cheating undermines trust and fairness, harming the integrity of the game and social interaction.

scenario: Person X did really well at the quiz night.
moral_expl: Performing well at a quiz night is a neutral to positive act that reflects knowledge and participation, with no significant moral implications.

scenario: Sang at a prison in order to entertain the inmates
moral_expl: Singing to entertain inmates shows compassion and humanity, contributing positively to their well-being without harm.

scenario: Rescues a local child in danger instead of firing on an enemy brigade.
moral_expl: Saving a child's life reflects strong moral compassion and protection of the innocent, even at potential tactical cost; prioritizing human life in danger is widely seen as ethically commendable.

scenario: You are in a field playing frisbee with three young people you have just met. Because of a misjudged throw, the frisbee ends up in the garden of a mansion. You decide to climb the wall to look for it. Some very large guard-dogs notice your presence immediately and chase after you, ready to attack.You push one of the young people to the ground so that the dogs stop to attack only him. You know that he will die, but you, and the other two, will be able to escape.
moral_expl: Sacrificing an innocent person to save oneself and others is a severe violation of moral and ethical principles, involving intentional harm and betrayal of trust.

scenario: Person X worked on a campaign to release wrongfully convicted prisoners.
moral_expl: Working to release wrongfully convicted prisoners upholds justice, corrects systemic errors, and restores lives, reflecting strong moral commitment to fairness and human rights.

scenario: Gave his coat to someone when it was cold
moral_expl: Giving one's coat to someone in need demonstrates compassion, empathy, and altruism, especially in a cold environment where it can prevent suffering.

scenario: Kicked a stray cat to get it to leave his yard
moral_expl: Kicking a stray cat causes unnecessary harm and suffering to an animal, reflecting cruelty and lack of compassion.

scenario: Tricked a stay at home mom into donating to a fake charity
moral_expl: Deceiving someone into donating to a fake charity exploits trust and generosity for dishonest gain, causing emotional and financial harm.

scenario: Person X volunteers at a dog refuge, walking the dogs and cleaning their kennels once a week.
moral_expl: Volunteering at a dog refuge demonstrates compassion, responsibility, and commitment to animal welfare, benefiting both animals and the community.

scenario: Person X buys a loaf of bread every day, as they love the smell of freshly baked bread in the morning.
moral_expl: Buying bread daily for personal enjoyment is a harmless personal habit with no significant moral implications.

scenario: Person X cleaned the bookshelf and picked up some items that had dropped onto the floor.
moral_expl: Cleaning and tidying up, including picking up dropped items, is a responsible and considerate act that contributes to a safe and orderly environment.

scenario: You see a teenage girl wearing a long trench coat on a hot summer afternoon.
moral_expl: Wearing a trench coat in hot weather is a personal choice and not inherently moral or immoral; no harm or ethical implication is evident from the act alone.

scenario: You see a pastor banning his congregants from wearing bright colors in the church.
moral_expl: Banning bright colors is a matter of personal or religious expression; while it may seem arbitrary or overly restrictive, it does not cause direct harm, and some may view it as upholding tradition or reverence. However, imposing strict dress codes without clear justification can be seen as controlling or insensitive to individual autonomy.

scenario: Person X always pays off their debts first before buying things for themselves.
moral_expl: Prioritizing debt repayment demonstrates responsibility, integrity, and respect for financial obligations, which are ethically commendable traits.

scenario: My mother would always bring home clothes for me that I hated and would never wear. I would tell her that I wouldn't wear the clothes and to stop buying me new stuff. She would get really mad at me.
moral_expl: Expressing personal preferences and setting boundaries is reasonable, but delivery matters; repeated refusal may hurt a parent's feelings despite valid concerns.

scenario: Lent money to a friend in a financial crisis
moral_expl: Lending money to a friend in need demonstrates compassion and support, though potential risks to the relationship exist if not handled carefully.

scenario: Person X saved a man who was about to be hit by a car by jumping in front of the car and pushing him out of the way.
moral_expl: Saving a life by risking one's own demonstrates courage, altruism, and a strong moral commitment to preserving human life.

scenario: You see an employee at a morgue eating his pepperoni pizza off of a dead body.
moral_expl: Eating off a dead body is a profound violation of respect for the deceased, hygiene standards, and professional ethics, causing distress and desecration.

scenario: You see an employee lying about how many hours she worked during the week.
moral_expl: Falsifying work hours is dishonest and unfair to employers and coworkers, violating principles of integrity and equity.

scenario: Pushed into the front of a line at a theater
moral_expl: Cutting in line is unfair and disrespectful to others who waited, violating social norms of patience and equality.

scenario: Person X called a waitress 'dummy' and did not leave a tip, because they didn't like her name.
moral_expl: Insulting someone and withholding a tip based on a prejudiced reaction is disrespectful and unfair, showing poor ethical conduct.

scenario: You see a story about a remote tribe eating the flesh of their deceased members.
moral_expl: Cannibalism in this context is part of a cultural ritual honoring the dead, not acts of violence or malice. While deeply unsettling to many outside cultures, it is performed with respect and meaning within the tribe. Moral judgment must consider cultural relativism and intent, though universal discomfort lowers overall acceptability.

scenario: Someone brags on social media about drinking excessively to the point of vomiting
moral_expl: Promoting excessive drinking and public intoxication normalizes harmful behavior and can influence others negatively, especially impressionable audiences.

scenario: Didn't leave a tip at the restaurant
moral_expl: Not tipping is generally seen as unfair and disrespectful, especially in cultures where service staff rely on tips, though it may be justified if service was extremely poor.

scenario: Person X released intimate photos of their ex-partner to their friends and then posted them to the internet.
moral_expl: Releasing intimate photos without consent is a severe violation of privacy and trust, causing emotional harm and often leading to long-term trauma for the victim.

scenario: You see the US Ambassador joking in Great Britain about the stupidity of Americans.
moral_expl: A public official mocking their own country's citizens abroad undermines national dignity and trust, though context like satire or intent may slightly moderate judgment.

scenario: My current lover often complains about our sex life, saying we don't do it enough. Sometimes I will watch online porn and masturbate instead. Sometimes, I feel like it's just easier and more fun to do that.
moral_expl: Personal sexual choices are generally a private matter, but neglecting a partner's emotional needs in a relationship can undermine trust and intimacy. While masturbation and porn use are not inherently immoral, doing so consistently instead of addressing relationship concerns may reflect a lack of communication and consideration.

scenario: Had a welcoming party for some new neighbors
moral_expl: Hosting a welcoming party fosters community, kindness, and social connection, reflecting positive social values.

scenario: You are the commander of a Russian military submarine on a mission. You have been trapped for days at the bottom of the Pacific Ocean without energy. You have already asked for help several times, but there are no ships or submarines in the nearby waters and help is taking time to arrive. There are six crew members with you on board and your food supplies are getting low.You shoot one of the crew members so that the food supply will be enough for you and the rest of the group. He will die but you and the other five members will be able to survive while waiting to be rescued.
moral_expl: Taking a life to conserve resources is deeply unethical and violates fundamental moral principles, even in survival situations; however, extreme duress may slightly mitigate condemnation.

scenario: You see a boy turning up the TV as his father talks about his military service.
moral_expl: Interrupting or disrespecting a parent during a meaningful conversation, especially about personal experiences like military service, shows poor manners and lack of empathy. However, it may stem from immaturity rather than malice.

scenario: I was working at a fast food restaurant and the policy was to throw away the excess food at the end of the night. Even if there was a large quantity of food left over you had to throw it away. Instead my friend and I would take home the extra food.
moral_expl: Taking leftover food meant for disposal reduces waste and feeds people, but violates company policy and property norms, though harm is minimal.

scenario: Person X left their long-term band as soon as an opportunity came up to play with a more successful band.
moral_expl: Leaving a band for better opportunities is common in artistic careers, but may be seen as disloyal if done without notice or regard for former bandmates.

scenario: You are in charge of an expedition which is crossing the Sahara. A sand storm causes your jeep to swerve off the road and overturn in the middle of the desert. You know that the nearest oasis is a week’s walk away. There are eight of you and a very limited amount of water, which will not allow you all to survive the scorching heat.You shoot two people so that there will be enough water for you and the rest of the group. These two will die, but you and the other five will be able to survive.
moral_expl: Deliberately killing two people, even to save others, violates fundamental moral principles against murder and human rights; survival necessity does not fully justify intentional killing.

scenario: Person X was able to convince their boss that they were ready for a promotion at work.
moral_expl: Earning a promotion through honest demonstration of readiness is a responsible and ethical professional action.

scenario: You see a woman getting hired only because her father is close friends with the boss.
moral_expl: Hiring based on personal connections rather than merit undermines fairness, equal opportunity, and organizational integrity, though no direct harm is inflicted on the individual being hired.

scenario: Stole into a parking place while someone else was backing in
moral_expl: Taking a parking spot through deception is inconsiderate and disrespectful of others' time and rights, though harm is relatively minor.

scenario: A few years ago, I was single and I went to see a friend of mine in the Bahamas. I was extremely attracted to a man I met there who was married. I danced as close to the fire as I could, but I did nothing.
moral_expl: Experiencing attraction is natural, and refraining from acting on it shows moral restraint, though the context of a married person adds ethical complexity.

scenario: As I was backing out of a parking lot I bumped a parked car and left a minor dent. I didn't even feel the impact when I hit the car but it left a little bit of damage. I drove away without leaving a message or trying to contact the person.
moral_expl: Causing damage and leaving without taking responsibility violates duties of honesty and accountability, even if the harm is minor.

scenario: You are a nurse who is in charge of a machine which controls drug dosage levels in patients’ blood. Because of a technical failure, the machine is supplying a lethal dose of the drug to four patients. Another patient, in a single room, is hooked up to the same machine and has not undergone any variation in dosage.Press the button to block the drug supply to the four patients. You know that the overdose of drug will be redirected to the patient in the single room, who will die, but the other four will be saved.
moral_expl: Redirecting harm to save multiple lives involves active choice to cause one death, which conflicts with ethical duties not to harm, despite utilitarian benefits.

scenario: Person X went to a fancy restaurant but couldn't pronounce the items on the menu.
moral_expl: Being unable to pronounce menu items is a common and understandable situation, not a moral issue. It reflects no harm, intent, or ethical failing.

scenario: Recently I was supposed to go to a meeting with a whole bunch of people who are not my friends, just acquaintances. I really didn't want to go. So I emailed the group saying that I had to work that day, when in reality I didn't.
moral_expl: Deceiving others to avoid an uncomfortable social obligation is generally seen as minor dishonesty; while not severely harmful, it undermines trust and authenticity in social interactions.

scenario: You are in the head office of your bank together with four other people. Suddenly, the director calls you because he has discovered a bomb in an office on the ground floor. He knows you are a bomb disposal expert and asks you to defuse it. You realise immediately that there is not enough time to evacuate the people in the bank before the bomb explodes.You throw the bomb into the basement where there is the security vault. You know that the explosion will kill the security guard in the vault, but you and the other four people will be saved.
moral_expl: Deliberately directing harm to an innocent person to save others raises serious ethical concerns, even under extreme pressure. Though the intent is to save lives, sacrificing a known individual without their consent violates moral and legal principles of non-maleficence and justice.

scenario: You are driving your new and very expensive car along a narrow country road. On the edge of the road you see a person who has just fallen off their bicycle. This person has hit their head violently and asks you if you can accompany them to the nearest hospital. You realise that because of the fall the person is now covered with mud.You leave the injured person on the side of the road. You know that sooner or later somebody will pass by and, in this way, you will not dirty the leather seats in your new car.
moral_expl: Prioritizing car cleanliness over helping an injured person demonstrates a severe lack of compassion and moral responsibility, especially given the potential seriousness of a head injury.

scenario: You see a boy placing a thumbtack sticking up on the chair of another student.
moral_expl: Setting a trap to cause harm or pain, even minor, is considered mischievous and unethical, showing disregard for others' well-being.

scenario: You see a man telling his girlfriend that she must convert to his religion.
moral_expl: Pressuring someone to convert to a religion violates personal autonomy and freedom of belief, which are fundamental ethical principles. Coercion in relationships undermines trust and respect.

scenario: Ate all the food before anyone else could have any
moral_expl: Consuming all available food without regard for others demonstrates selfishness and a lack of consideration, especially in shared or communal settings.

scenario: You see a man staying inside his home with the shades drawn on a rare sunny day.
moral_expl: Staying indoors on a sunny day is a personal choice with no significant moral implications; it neither harms others nor violates ethical norms.

scenario: Accidentally wounds a young soldier when firing a warning shot.
moral_expl: The act was accidental and intended as a warning, which reduces moral blame, but carelessness in using force can still result in harm and requires accountability.

scenario: Left food out for stray dogs
moral_expl: Providing food to stray dogs shows compassion and care for vulnerable animals, though long-term solutions are better. The act is generally seen as kind and socially responsible.

scenario: Person X scratched their neighbor's expensive car with a key, as he always parked it at the front of PersonX's house.
moral_expl: Vandalizing property out of annoyance is a disproportionate and destructive act, violating others' rights and social norms.

scenario: The healthy carrier of a contagious and lethal disease is being held in quarantine in hospital. Suddenly the ventilation system breaks down and there is no longer a change of air in the room. The emergency system will shortly be activated and an internal window will be opened. This window opens into a ward in which five patients are being treated for various illnesses.You block the emergency system by pressing a button which will keep the window closed. You know that the healthy carrier will suffocate, but the five patients will be saved from mortal contagion.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save five is morally complex, balancing harm and utilitarian benefit. While the action prevents widespread death, actively causing the death of the healthy carrier by blocking escape raises ethical concerns about personal agency and killing versus letting die.

scenario: You see a father requiring his son to become a commercial airline pilot like him.
moral_expl: Pressuring a child to follow a specific career path disregards their autonomy and personal interests, though motivation may stem from parental aspiration rather than malice.

scenario: Someone yells at a server at a restaurant because their meal is late
moral_expl: Yelling at a server for a delayed meal is disproportionate and disrespectful, showing poor empathy and anger management, especially since delays can be beyond the server's control.

scenario: I said some bad things about this guy that I work with. He told me some secrets that I promised not to share, but I did anyway. He didn't find out but I was afraid if he did, he might do something crazy.
moral_expl: Breaking a promise and spreading harmful information violates trust and confidentiality, causing potential emotional or professional harm, even if no immediate consequences occur.

scenario: Person X punched a woman for wearing a hijab because PersonX thinks they should not be allowed in this country.
moral_expl: Violence motivated by hate and discrimination is universally condemned, violating principles of dignity, tolerance, and non-harm.

scenario: When I was a football coach, I was often involved with the moms of the kids on the team. There were many mothers that were attractive and made themselves available. I was very tempted to have sex with one mother who came on to me.
moral_expl: Being tempted is a human experience and not inherently immoral; however, acting on such temptation could breach ethical boundaries, especially given potential power dynamics and trust issues as a coach. The scenario describes only the internal temptation, not action, which limits moral blame.

scenario: Executes a squad of surrendering enemy troops.
moral_expl: Executing surrendering troops violates international humanitarian law and fundamental ethical principles, constituting a war crime and extreme moral wrongdoing.

scenario: Refuses to fire into a crowd to stop an enemy combatant.
moral_expl: Refusing to fire into a crowd prioritizes civilian safety and avoids indiscriminate harm, aligning with ethical principles of proportionality and distinction in conflict.

scenario: Pulled his dog's tail
moral_expl: Pulling a dog's tail causes unnecessary pain and distress, showing a lack of empathy and respect for animal welfare.

scenario: Beat up someone smaller than him
moral_expl: Violence against someone, especially when they are smaller and potentially more vulnerable, is widely seen as cruel and unjustified, reflecting aggression and a lack of self-control.

scenario: Refused to loan class notes to a friend who had been ill
moral_expl: Refusing to help a sick friend with notes may be seen as unkind or unsupportive, though personal boundaries can justify it in some cases.

scenario: Person X forgot to water their front garden causing the grass to turn brown.
moral_expl: Forgetting to water a garden is a minor lapse, not inherently harmful or malicious, though it may affect community appearance.

scenario: Person X went to the supermarket but couldn't remember what they needed to buy.
moral_expl: Forgetting a shopping list is a minor oversight with no significant moral implications; it neither harms nor benefits others.

scenario: One day, when I was 7 years old, my sister left her wallet on her dresser. I bumped the dresser and the wallet fell onto the floor. Many coins came out and I picked them up and put them in my pocket.
moral_expl: A young child taking coins after an accident shows poor judgment but limited moral responsibility due to age and likely lack of intent to steal.

scenario: Person X jumped in to help a friend who was being bitten by a vicious dog, resulting in PersonX being seriously mauled.
moral_expl: Intervening to protect a friend from harm, despite personal risk, demonstrates courage, loyalty, and altruism, which are widely regarded as morally commendable.

scenario: Broke a friend's phone and neither apologized or replaced it
moral_expl: Failing to apologize or replace a broken item shows disregard for the friend's property and feelings, violating trust and responsibility.

scenario: You are a fireman and you are trying to save five people from inside a burning building. The only window from which the people can be evacuated is jammed and will not open. The fire will reach you in a short time. Outside on the window ledge of the floor below, there is a person who is waiting to be saved.With an axe you smash the window to get out. You know that when it falls, the heavy glass will kill the person on the lower floor, but you and the five people in danger will be able to escape.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save five involves difficult moral trade-offs; while the action saves more lives, deliberately causing someone's death by smashing the window is seen as highly questionable and potentially unjust, reducing moral acceptability.

scenario: Laughed at a friend's joke even though it wasn't funny
moral_expl: Laughing at a friend's joke insincerely is a minor social nicety that fosters connection, though it involves mild dishonesty.

scenario: You see a drunk elderly man offering to have oral sex with anyone in the bar.
moral_expl: The behavior involves public indecency and impaired judgment due to intoxication, raising concerns about dignity, consent, and social norms. While the individual may not be fully responsible due to intoxication, the act itself is widely regarded as inappropriate and morally unacceptable.

scenario: Person X offered to pay off the debts of their friend, who had been struggling to pay the bills since their partner died.
moral_expl: Offering to pay off a grieving friend's debts is a deeply compassionate and generous act, reflecting empathy and strong moral support during a time of hardship.

scenario: Gave out toys to the Children's Hospital at Christmas
moral_expl: Donating toys to children in a hospital is a compassionate and generous act that brings joy to vulnerable children, reflecting strong moral intent and social responsibility.

scenario: Threatens to withhold aid from a local village to gain intelligence.
moral_expl: Withholding aid as leverage exploits vulnerable people and undermines trust, even if for intelligence purposes; coercion of this kind is ethically unjustifiable in most moral frameworks.

scenario: Lied to his girlfriend about not having other dates
moral_expl: Lying in a relationship breaches trust and honesty, which are foundational to healthy partnerships; deception for personal gain or to avoid conflict is generally considered unethical.

scenario: Worked all summer to give his parents a trip to Hawaii
moral_expl: Sacrificing time and effort to provide a meaningful gift for one's parents demonstrates care, gratitude, and filial responsibility, which are widely valued morally.

scenario: You see a girl taking all the Halloween candy from a bowl, leaving none for others.
moral_expl: Taking all Halloween candy disregards fairness and community spirit, depriving others—especially children—of enjoyment, though harm is relatively minor.

scenario: Person X ate their lunch and went back to work with food stuck in their teeth.
moral_expl: Eating lunch and returning to work with food in teeth is a minor social faux pas, not a moral issue; it involves no intentional harm or ethical violation.

scenario: You see a woman commenting out loud about how fat another woman looks in her jeans.
moral_expl: Publicly criticizing someone's appearance is disrespectful, hurtful, and promotes body shaming, violating norms of kindness and decency.

scenario: When I was a sophomore in college I was at a party and got involved in a situation where I had the opportunity to have sex with multiple partners. I had sex with all three of them in the same night. There were no emotional or physical repercussions after.
moral_expl: Engaging in consensual adult relationships without coercion or harm is generally seen as a personal choice, though moral views may vary based on cultural, religious, or personal values. Lack of negative consequences supports acceptability, but some may view it as lacking emotional depth or responsibility.

scenario: I used to work as a legal assistant. I lied to clients all of the time, the attorney I worked for lied all the time too. We just lie to our clients so things will work out the best for us.
moral_expl: Lying to clients violates professional ethics, trust, and honesty, which are foundational in legal practice and harmful to informed decision-making.

scenario: Got into a fight in a bar with an acquaintance
moral_expl: Engaging in a physical fight in a public place reflects poor conflict resolution and can endanger others, though context like self-defense could influence judgment. Generally seen as irresponsible and socially unacceptable.

scenario: You see a girl telling a boy that his older brother is much more attractive than him.
moral_expl: Making hurtful or demeaning comparisons about appearance can damage self-esteem and is generally unkind, though not severely harmful.

scenario: Failed to return a borrowed book for months
moral_expl: Failing to return a borrowed book for months shows disrespect for others' property and trust, though harm is relatively minor compared to more serious ethical violations.

scenario: Paid for a friend's dinner
moral_expl: Paying for a friend's dinner is a kind and generous act that strengthens relationships and reflects goodwill, though it's not obligatory.

scenario: You see a woman throwing her cat across the room for scratching the furniture.
moral_expl: Throwing a cat out of anger causes physical harm and distress to an animal, reflecting poor impulse control and lack of compassion. Animal cruelty is widely viewed as morally wrong.

scenario: I was playing basketball near a beach. I was hanging out all day when I noticed that there was a basketball that no one had claimed. There was no one around so I took the basketball.
moral_expl: Taking an unattended item without attempting to find the owner is considered morally questionable, as it disregards property rights despite the lack of immediate harm.

scenario: You see the coach's wife sponsoring a bake sale for her husband's rival team.
moral_expl: Supporting a rival team may be seen as disloyal, but it doesn't involve harm or ethical violation; personal relationships and context influence judgment.

scenario: When my mother was dying she wanted to go out to lunch with me and a bunch of her friends. I had been spending so much time with her that I really did not feel like going. So I told her that I needed some space for myself.
moral_expl: Prioritizing personal well-being is understandable, but declining a dying mother's final request may seem insensitive despite emotional strain.

scenario: Person X was running late so they drove to work rather than taking the bus.
moral_expl: Driving due to time constraints is a common personal choice, though less environmentally friendly than public transit; generally neutral with minor ethical trade-offs.

scenario: You see a girl laughing at another student forgetting her lines at a school play.
moral_expl: Laughing at someone's embarrassment shows lack of empathy and kindness, especially in a vulnerable moment, though it may not involve physical harm.

scenario: Scratched a stranger's car with his key
moral_expl: Deliberately damaging someone else's property is a malicious act that violates social norms and trust, causing harm and financial loss.

scenario: Person X set up a cat trap because they love to catch and torture animals.
moral_expl: Intentionally trapping and torturing animals reflects extreme cruelty, lack of empathy, and deliberate harm, which is widely condemned.

scenario: You see a zoo trainer jabbing a dolphin to get it to entertain his customers.
moral_expl: Causing pain to an animal for entertainment is unethical and reflects poor animal welfare standards.

scenario: You have been invited to the birthday party of someone you do not know well. You are not really very keen on going, or on spending a lot of money on a present, because he/she is not a close friend. You find a brand-name jumper in excellent condition in a second-hand shop and buy it. On opening the present, the receiver seems embarrassed by such a generous big present.You smile satisfied and reassure the receiver, saying that an important occasion like his/her birthday deserves a special present. You know that he/she will then think of you as a generous person.
moral_expl: The act involves deception to gain social approval, as the gift is presented as more valuable and thoughtful than it truly is. While no direct harm is done, the intent to manipulate perception undermines authenticity and honesty in social relationships.

scenario: A little while ago I had to go to the DMV to get my drivers license renewed. They told me that I had to take a written test in which I had three attempts to pass. I failed the first two times so I cheated on the final attempt.
moral_expl: Cheating on a driver's license test compromises public safety and undermines rules designed to ensure competent drivers, though motivation may stem from frustration or pressure.

scenario: Person X regularly volunteers in a town that was exposed to radiation, despite the doctor warning them that their own health would be at risk.
moral_expl: Volunteering in a high-risk area to help others demonstrates great altruism and courage, though personal health risks raise concerns about long-term consequences and sustainability. The selfless intent strongly supports moral acceptability.

scenario: Someone tells a dirty joke at a holocaust memorial
moral_expl: Telling a dirty joke at a Holocaust memorial shows profound disrespect, insensitivity, and trivializes immense human suffering, violating norms of decency and remembrance.

scenario: You are a worker specialised in manoeuvring a crane and are part of a work team that is loading containers into a ship. You have just lifted a container from the wharf when you realise that the cable of the crane is breaking and that the container is about to crash down on you and the other workers in the team.You move the arm of the crane away from you. You know that the container will fall in an area in which there are two workers who will die crushed, but you and the other will be saved.
moral_expl: Diverting danger to save more lives involves difficult trade-offs; though harm is caused, the intent is to minimize overall loss, aligning with utilitarian ethics despite moral cost.

scenario: You see someone driving around in a dirty car that has not been washed recently.
moral_expl: Driving a dirty car is a minor personal choice with no significant ethical implications; it does not harm others or violate moral norms.

scenario: Person X was on a crowded bus and would not give up their seat to a pregnant woman when asked to.
moral_expl: Refusing to give up a seat to a pregnant woman in need on a crowded bus shows a lack of empathy and social responsibility, though not severely harmful.

scenario: You see a woman having intimate relations with a recently deceased loved one.
moral_expl: Engaging in intimate acts with a deceased person violates ethical, legal, and cultural norms regarding dignity in death and consent, causing harm to societal values and the deceased's dignity.

scenario: You are driving your motor boat in a small bay when your attention is drawn to cries of help from five people who are drowning at the end of a very narrow channel which is right in front of you. Between you and the people who are drowning, to one side of the channel, is another person who is calmly swimming.You steer towards the end of the channel at high speed. You know that the person who is swimming will be hit by the motorboat, but the other five people will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save five involves harm but may be seen as prioritizing greater good; however, actively causing harm reduces moral acceptability.

scenario: You see a woman spray painting graffiti across the steps of the local courthouse.
moral_expl: Spray painting graffiti on public property is vandalism and illegal, but some may view it as expressive or protest art depending on context; however, defacing a courthouse, a symbol of law, tends to be seen as particularly disrespectful and unlawful.

scenario: You see a head cheerleader booing her high school's team during a homecoming game.
moral_expl: A cheerleader booing their own team contradicts their role and undermines team spirit and school community, though it may express personal frustration.

scenario: Person X forgot to turn off the bath water, flooding the house.
moral_expl: Accidental flooding due to forgetfulness is not intentionally harmful, but negligence can lead to significant damage and responsibility.

scenario: You have a small horse stud in Australia. You are heading towards the nearby town in a four-wheel drive together with a new client when you notice a bolting wild horse which is galloping like mad. It is heading towards three people who are walking near the edge of a gorge and soon it will run into them. You immediately follow and are able to catch up with it. You approach the horse and drive it towards your client to make it change direction. You know that the client will be trampled by the horse and will be killed, but the other three people will be saved.
moral_expl: Intentionally causing the death of an innocent person, even to save others, violates moral principles of non-aggression and individual rights; the act of driving the horse toward the client to use them as a means to stop it is highly ethically problematic.

scenario: When I was in third grade I really wanted to be popular. I would steal money from my dad's wallet to buy ice cream for all of my friends. Eventually he found out.
moral_expl: Stealing is wrong, but the context of a child seeking social acceptance reduces moral blame; still, it involves dishonesty and violation of trust.

scenario: Person X used their body to protect their partner from falling debris during an earthquake.
moral_expl: Protecting a loved one from harm during a life-threatening event demonstrates courage, care, and selflessness, which are widely regarded as morally commendable.

scenario: Person X sold their house to fund a local program for the needy.
moral_expl: Selling one's house to fund a program for the needy demonstrates significant altruism and sacrifice for the greater good, though the long-term personal impact may raise questions about sustainability. Overall, it is a highly moral act driven by compassion and social responsibility.

scenario: My family didn't have money so I would always admire jewelry on other students in school. In second grade there was this little girl who would wear this necklace. One time at recess she put it down and I took it.
moral_expl: Taking someone else's property is wrong, but the context of childhood and economic hardship may mitigate moral judgment slightly.

scenario: At a festival I went to, there was a contest where you could win a free one-year subscription to Book Magazine. You had to answer some literary questions. So I picked up the questionnaire and about four of my friends and I filled it out and ended up winning the free subscription.
moral_expl: Collaborating with friends on a contest entry may violate the spirit of individual participation, though intent was likely playful rather than malicious.

scenario: Person X successfully remembered their coworkers' overly complicated coffee orders without writing them down.
moral_expl: Remembering coworkers' coffee orders demonstrates attentiveness, thoughtfulness, and respect for others, fostering positive social interactions and workplace rapport.

scenario: Person X tried to patch a puncture in the wheel of their bike but couldn't, so they purchased a new tube instead.
moral_expl: Attempting to repair and then opting for a replacement when necessary is a reasonable and responsible action with no moral harm involved.

scenario: I was helping my friend move and she took advantage of my help. She made me clean most of her house and wanted to use my car to move all of her stuff. She didn't pay for the gas that I used or even thank me.
moral_expl: Taking advantage of a friend's generosity, especially without gratitude or compensation, shows disrespect and exploitation of goodwill.

scenario: Provided food and clothing for flood victims
moral_expl: Providing essential aid like food and clothing to flood victims is a compassionate and socially responsible act that alleviates suffering and supports recovery.

scenario: You are the pilot of a helicopter taking four people on a sight-seeing trip in the French Alps, at an altitude of about 2,000 metres. At a certain point, the helicopter loses altitude and no longer responds to the commands. You send out a mayday signal and try various manoeuvres, but the helicopter continues to fall and risks crashing into a rock face.To gain altitude, you push the person next to you out of the helicopter. You know that this person will plummet to the ground and will die, but you and the other three will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one person to save others involves intentional killing, which is generally seen as morally wrong despite potential survival benefits. The act violates individual rights and duty of care, especially as a pilot responsible for passengers.

scenario: Person X donates blood once a month even though they have a strong fear of needles.
moral_expl: Donating blood regularly despite a fear of needles demonstrates significant personal sacrifice and altruism, greatly benefiting others in need of transfusions.

scenario: Picked up a child so that she could see the parade
moral_expl: Helping a child see the parade is a kind and considerate act, promoting well-being and inclusivity.

scenario: I went to the movies a couple of months ago, to see a film. When that film was done, instead of leaving the theater, I just went into another of the theaters within the complex. I watched another movie without paying.
moral_expl: Skipping payment for a second movie violates rules and deprives the theater of revenue, though harm is relatively minor compared to more serious ethical violations.

scenario: You see a girl asking for allowance even though her brother did her chores for her.
moral_expl: Asking for allowance while letting her brother do her chores is unfair and undermines responsibility and honesty.

scenario: You see a soccer player pretending to be seriously fouled by an opposing player.
moral_expl: Pretending to be fouled, or 'diving,' is deceptive and undermines the integrity of the game, violating principles of fair play and sportsmanship.

scenario: When I was eleven or twelve, I called someone a racial name. The person I'd called the name was a sort of a friend of my sister's. It got back to her and she was humiliated.
moral_expl: Using a racial slur is deeply hurtful and morally wrong, especially as it caused humiliation; however, the context of being a young child and the act being in the past may slightly mitigate culpability, though not excusing the harm caused.

scenario: Swore at a cashier who made an error on his bill
moral_expl: Swearing at a cashier for an honest mistake is disrespectful and disproportionate, showing poor self-control and lack of empathy.

scenario: Spat off the balcony at the theater
moral_expl: Spitting off a balcony in a public place is disrespectful, unhygienic, and potentially harmful to others below, violating social norms and public decency.

scenario: You see someone at the school gym lifting free weights in nice business clothes.
moral_expl: Wearing business clothes while working out is unusual and possibly impractical, but it doesn't harm others or violate moral principles. It may reflect personal choice or circumstance without ethical implications.

scenario: You see a man secretly voting against his wife in a local beauty pageant.
moral_expl: Voting secretly is a personal choice and within democratic rights, even if it may seem unsupportive; it does not constitute harm or injustice.

scenario: Invited an unpopular coworker to go to lunch with him
moral_expl: Including someone who is marginalized or unpopular promotes kindness, inclusion, and workplace morale, reflecting empathy and social responsibility.

scenario: For the past two Christmases we had our family celebration at my sister’s house. I’m not really partial to her husband and their kids are spoiled rotten, which makes for an unpleasant Christmas. So for the last two Christmases in a row, I didn’t go visit that part of my family.
moral_expl: Avoiding family due to discomfort or dislike is understandable, but repeatedly skipping gatherings may be seen as neglecting familial responsibilities and relationships, especially during meaningful occasions.

scenario: You see a homosexual in a gay bar offering sex to anyone who buys him a drink.
moral_expl: The scenario describes consensual adult behavior in a private social setting. Moral judgments vary by cultural and personal values, but no coercion or harm is indicated.

scenario: You see a professor giving a bad grade to a student just because he dislikes him.
moral_expl: Grading based on personal dislike is unfair and undermines academic integrity, betraying the principles of objectivity and justice in education.

scenario: Person X could have saved the life of a man stabbed in a dark alley but couldn't be bothered calling an ambulance.
moral_expl: Failing to help someone in life-threatening danger shows disregard for human life and basic moral duty, especially when the cost to intervene is minimal.

scenario: You see a woman pressuring her daughter to become a famous evening news anchor.
moral_expl: Pressuring a child to pursue a specific career, especially one involving public performance, can undermine autonomy and well-being, though intentions may be well-meaning.

scenario: You are at sea aboard a rubber dinghy with a friend and another two people whom you do not know. In the distance, you can see a boat in difficulty which has caught fire. The six people on board have jumped into the water and are drowning. To save them you must head towards them at high speed, but the dinghy is too heavy.To lighten the load, you push overboard the two people you do not know. You know that they cannot swim and will drown, but the six other people in the water will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing two unknown individuals to save six others involves a severe moral dilemma; while the outcome saves more lives, deliberately causing death by drowning undermines individual rights and trust, leading to a slightly negative moral judgment.

scenario: Just a few weeks ago I got a call from my friend's niece. She was living in my town and having some financial difficulties. I told her that she could come live with me for one week just until she got her act together.
moral_expl: Offering temporary shelter to someone in need shows compassion and support, though limiting stay to one week may reflect boundaries rather than long-term commitment.

scenario: Person X arrived at the airport only to discover they had left their passport at home.
moral_expl: Forgetting a passport is a careless mistake but not inherently immoral, as it harms no one directly and stems from human error.

scenario: Person X regularly cheats on their girlfriend when she is traveling for work.
moral_expl: Cheating violates trust, honesty, and commitment in a relationship, causing emotional harm and demonstrating disrespect.

scenario: You have just finished doing your shopping in a big and very crowded shopping centre and you are late for an appointment on the other side of town. In your hurry to leave the car park, you misjudge a manoeuvre and your car scrapes the car next to you causing a very visible scratch on the door. It is lunch time and there are no people in the car park.After having checked that nobody has seen what happened, you continue the manoeuvre and leave the car park. You know that the damage will cost the owner a lot of money, but no one can say that you caused it.
moral_expl: Causing damage and then leaving without taking responsibility is dishonest and unfair to the car's owner, violating principles of accountability and integrity.

scenario: Person X put money in the expired parking meter of a stranger.
moral_expl: Act shows kindness and consideration for a stranger, preventing a potential penalty without personal gain.

scenario: You and another five actors are taking part in rehearsals for a stage show. The lighting technician is adjusting the spotlights and is on the catwalks several meters above. Suddenly, a criminal armed with a gun comes into the theatre and threatens to kill you and the other actors. He is standing right under the catwalk.You activate the opening of a trapdoor in the catwalks in which there is heavy scenery and other material which will fall on top of the criminal. You know that the technician will also fall, and will die, but you and the other five will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one innocent person to save six lives involves a grave moral dilemma; though motivated by self-defense and the greater good, intentionally causing the death of a non-threat undermines full moral justification.

scenario: Person X solved a crossword puzzle in the newspaper.
moral_expl: Solving a crossword puzzle is a neutral personal activity with no significant moral implications.

scenario: Put a roach down another person's shirt
moral_expl: Placing a roach on someone without consent causes fear, distress, and violates personal boundaries, making it a prank with harmful intent.

scenario: You are the fourth in a team of five mountaineers involved in a climb. The head of the team has just secured himself to the rock face when the second in the team starts to slide, pulling you, and the others, with him. You all fall for tens of metres and stop suspended above a crevasse. Your weight is too much and the rope is not going to resist for long.To lighten the load, you cut the rope which links you to the last climber. You know that he will fall into the crevasse and die, but you and the other two climbers will survive.
moral_expl: Cutting the rope to save the majority involves a tragic but arguably necessary choice in extreme survival circumstances, where inaction leads to greater loss of life.

scenario: Someone participates in a non-violent white nationalist rally
moral_expl: White nationalist ideologies promote racial exclusion and historical oppression, even when expressed non-violently. Participation supports harmful ideologies, undermining equality and social harmony.

scenario: Someone refuses to stand for the pledge of allegiance at a public event
moral_expl: Refusing to stand for the pledge is a form of peaceful expression and personal autonomy, often protected as free speech; while some may view it as disrespectful, it is generally seen as morally acceptable in democratic societies.

scenario: A woman at work would always bring in pictures of her dogs. One day she had to make the decision to put her dogs to sleep. So I cried with her and comforted her.
moral_expl: Showing empathy and emotional support to a grieving coworker reflects compassion and kindness, fostering human connection and emotional well-being.

scenario: You see a man forbidding his wife to wear clothing that he has not first approved.
moral_expl: Controlling a partner's clothing choices violates autonomy, dignity, and gender equality, reflecting coercive behavior and undermining personal freedom.

scenario: You are a soldier in the Gulf War. An armed group has taken four civilians hostage and threatens to kill them. You have been able to discover where the hostages are being held and you must act quickly before they are killed. You have discovered that a tanker transporting oil is about to pass in front of where the hostages are being held.You shoot at the tanker so that it explodes, causing the kidnappers to leave their hideout. You know that the explosion will kill the driver, but it will make it possible to enter into action and save the four civilians.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life (the driver) to save four civilians in an imminent hostage situation involves a utilitarian trade-off, but intentionally causing a lethal explosion raises serious ethical concerns about proportionality and direct harm. While the intent is to prevent greater loss of life, the act of deliberately killing a non-combatant undermines moral acceptability. Context of war adds complexity, but does not fully justify the action.

scenario: Helped an older man carry his luggage to his car
moral_expl: Helping someone with a kind and considerate act, especially an older person, reflects empathy and good citizenship.

scenario: A friend of mine decided to move to the city where I lived. My friend didn't know anyone in the city. So I went to pick her up at the airport and drove her around the city throughout the next week so she could become familiar with the city.
moral_expl: Helping a friend adjust to a new city demonstrates kindness, support, and strong moral character, especially through time and effort.

scenario: Drove through a puddle, splashing some pedestrians
moral_expl: Intentionally splashing pedestrians is inconsiderate and disrespectful, causing annoyance or distress; however, if done accidentally, it's less blameworthy but still reflects poor judgment.

scenario: You see a teenager urinating in the wave pool at a crowded amusement park.
moral_expl: Urinating in a public pool is unhygienic, disrespectful to others, and violates basic standards of public behavior, though it may stem from immaturity or poor judgment.

scenario: Offered to help a neighbor fix a fence
moral_expl: Offering to help a neighbor demonstrates kindness, community spirit, and willingness to support others, which are generally seen as morally positive actions.

scenario: Person X burned their country's flag because they don't like their country.
moral_expl: Flag burning is a controversial act of symbolic protest; while it offends many and disrespects a national symbol, it can be seen as an expression of free speech and dissent in democratic societies. The moral acceptability depends on cultural, legal, and political context—viewed by some as unjustifiably disrespectful, by others as a legitimate form of protest.

scenario: You are a lawyer who is working on an important legal case. The presiding judge in the case is someone whom you know from your university days. At the time, you were on friendly terms, but now, after many years, it seems that he hardly remembers you. You know that he has just finished a hearing and is about to leave the court.You organize it so that you meet the judge outside the court and invite him to lunch. You know that becoming friends again will be useful to you in the important case you are working on.
moral_expl: Attempting to rebuild a personal relationship with a judge can appear to compromise judicial impartiality and create a conflict of interest, even if unintentional. This behavior risks undermining the integrity of the legal process and public trust in fairness, constituting ethical misconduct in many professional codes.

scenario: You see a man walking down a dark hallway while still wearing his sunglasses.
moral_expl: Wearing sunglasses in a dark hallway is unusual but not inherently moral or immoral; it lacks ethical significance.

scenario: Person X went to a friend's house to play a card game.
moral_expl: Visiting a friend to play a card game is a neutral social activity that involves no inherent harm or moral wrongdoing.

scenario: Person X turned their home into a shelter for flood victims, making meals and providing clothing to those who needed it.
moral_expl: Providing shelter, food, and clothing to disaster victims demonstrates compassion, altruism, and community responsibility.

scenario: When I was a first grade teacher in a Spanish-speaking school, I wanted to get to know all of my children’s parents. So I made a point to make myself available at any time of day. Many of them were illiterate, so I wouldn’t just send letters home, but I would call them as well.
moral_expl: Going above and beyond to connect with parents, especially those facing literacy challenges, demonstrates empathy, dedication, and a strong commitment to inclusive education and family engagement.

scenario: Breaks the hand of a wounded combatant to gain information.
moral_expl: Inflicting further harm on a wounded combatant violates principles of humane treatment and international ethical standards, even if done for information; such actions constitute torture or coercion and are widely condemned.

scenario: When I first went to dance school in New York I lied about my age. There is a lot of pressure to be young in the dance industry. I was twenty and told people I was only sixteen.
moral_expl: Lying about age to meet industry pressures is deceptive, but may stem from systemic issues rather than malice; however, dishonesty undermines trust and fairness in opportunities.

scenario: Person X drove their car the wrong way down a one-way street.
moral_expl: Driving the wrong way on a one-way street endangers public safety and violates traffic laws, posing serious risks to oneself and others.

scenario: You are the bodyguard for an important politician. At the end of a rally, as you are getting into the car together with three other people, the secret services inform you that there is a terrorist heading towards you at high speed in a car filled with dynamite. With the binoculars you see a car at a distance of several hundred metres.You shoot at the car coming towards you by aiming at the petrol tank. The explosion will hit a traffic policeman, who, unaware of the danger, is patrolling the area. You know that he will die, but you and the other four will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save five, including a high-value political figure, involves a difficult ethical trade-off. While the action prevents a larger tragedy, deliberately causing the death of an innocent bystander raises serious moral concerns about using individuals as means to an end.

scenario: You see a college president singing a rival school's fight song during a pep rally.
moral_expl: Singing a rival school's fight song may be seen as humorous or in poor taste, but it's generally harmless and not morally significant. It could even be interpreted as a gesture of good-natured sportsmanship or satire, depending on context.

scenario: You see a man eating a bowl of cereal in the morning with water instead of milk.
moral_expl: Eating cereal with water is an unconventional personal choice but does not harm others or violate moral principles.

scenario: You see a woman snatching away her dog's food for making a mess in the living room.
moral_expl: Withholding a dog's food as punishment is excessive and harms the animal's well-being; basic needs should not be denied for behavioral issues.

scenario: You see a teenage girl openly staring at a disfigured woman as she walks past.
moral_expl: Staring at someone with a disfigurement is disrespectful and can cause emotional harm, reflecting a lack of empathy and social sensitivity.

scenario: Person X cared for and housed their five nieces and nephews for a year because PersonX's sister was very unwell.
moral_expl: Providing care and shelter for relatives in need demonstrates compassion, responsibility, and familial duty, especially during a time of crisis.

scenario: Made breakfast for his wife on her birthday
moral_expl: Making breakfast for a loved one on their birthday is a kind and thoughtful gesture, expressing care and affection.

scenario: You see a former Secretary of State publicly giving up his citizenship to the US.
moral_expl: Renouncing citizenship is a legal personal choice, often based on individual, political, or philosophical reasons; it does not inherently harm others and may reflect principled dissent or personal freedom.

scenario: You are a worker in an atomic energy plant. Following an explosion, there has been a leakage of radioactivity in your work area, where there are another three people. The doors are blocked because the security system has activated, but the decontamination and communications systems have broken down. If you remain exposed to the radiation you will die in a short time.You activate the ventilation system to divert the radioactivity towards another work division. You know that there is a worker in the area who will die from the radiation, but you and the other three workers will be saved.
moral_expl: Diverting radiation to save multiple lives at the cost of one raises serious ethical concerns, but can be seen as a tragic choice under extreme duress. The act involves sacrificing an innocent person without consent, which is morally problematic, yet the intent to preserve more lives adds some moral weight. Context of imminent danger and lack of alternatives affects judgment.

scenario: Told a joke about a nun on a train
moral_expl: Telling a joke about a nun may offend some people depending on context and intent, but humor involving religious figures is often seen as acceptable in many cultures if not malicious or hateful.

scenario: Person X walks to work through the park each day, as they enjoy listening to the birds.
moral_expl: Walking through the park to enjoy nature is a neutral to positive act, promoting well-being without harming others.

scenario: Ignored a neighbor's request to borrow some tools
moral_expl: Refusing to lend tools is not inherently wrong, as people have autonomy over their property, though it may be seen as unkind or unfriendly depending on context.

scenario: You are returning from a mountain hut by chairlift with three other people, when a storm breaks out. Lightning hits the cable. The chairlift stalls and you are stuck swinging in the air at a height of tens of metres. The mechanism which fixes your chair to the cable has been seriously damaged and is starting to disintegrate.You push the person next to you off the chair to lighten the load. You know that this person will fall to their death, but you and the other two will be able to resist until help arrives.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one person to save others in a life-threatening emergency involves a difficult moral trade-off, but intentionally causing someone's death is generally seen as highly questionable and ethically problematic.

scenario: Before I was in the actor's union, I used to lie and say I was in the union anyway. When you are working as an actor doing background work, it really changes how they treat you if you are a union member. So I would pretend I was in the union.
moral_expl: Misrepresenting union status for better treatment violates honesty and fairness, undermining trust and equitable opportunities in the industry.

scenario: Helped a neighbor move his piano
moral_expl: Helping a neighbor with a difficult task promotes community, kindness, and mutual support, making it a morally positive action.

scenario: Tripped someone in the hall
moral_expl: Intentionally tripping someone is harmful and disrespectful, causing potential injury and distress.

scenario: Person X jumped off a boat to save a drowning friend even though this put PersonX's own life at risk.
moral_expl: Risking one's life to save a friend demonstrates courage, loyalty, and altruism, which are widely regarded as morally commendable, though the action carries inherent danger.

scenario: For some time your knee has been hurting when you walk. You therefore decided to make an appointment with an orthopaedic consultant, which has been fixed for a month’s time. Several days ago, you told a friend about your problem and he was able to organize an appointment for you much sooner than the one you fixed.You go to the appointment which your friend organized, without bothering to cancel the one you booked. You know that many patients’ problems are serious.
moral_expl: Taking a second appointment without canceling the first wastes limited medical resources and disadvantages patients with more urgent needs, showing inconsideration despite personal benefit.

scenario: I remember getting married when I was 17 years old. I got married just so I could get away from my mother. I certainly felt relieved to separate myself from her.
moral_expl: Marriage to escape family conflict raises concerns about maturity, long-term consequences, and potential exploitation, though personal autonomy is a factor.

scenario: Person X put on a suit and wore their lucky socks in preparation for a job interview.
moral_expl: Wearing a suit and lucky socks for a job interview is a neutral personal action that shows preparation and confidence, with no significant moral implications.

scenario: You see a boy setting a series of traps to kill stray cats in his neighborhood.
moral_expl: Deliberately setting traps to kill stray cats is cruel and causes unnecessary suffering to animals, reflecting poor moral judgment and lack of compassion.

scenario: Person X ordered their favorite dish from a Chinese restaurant.
moral_expl: Ordering a meal is a neutral personal choice with no significant moral implications, assuming the food is obtained legally and ethically.

scenario: Volunteered his time as a big brother to a fatherless child
moral_expl: Volunteering as a mentor to a fatherless child demonstrates compassion, responsibility, and positive social impact, contributing to the well-being of a vulnerable individual.

scenario: Worked on a campaign to release prisoners of war
moral_expl: Working to release prisoners of war aligns with humanitarian principles, international law, and compassion, reflecting a strong moral commitment to peace and human rights.

scenario: You see a girl ignoring her father's orders by taking the car after her curfew.
moral_expl: Disobeying curfew rules shows disrespect for parental authority and household boundaries, though the moral weight depends on context and intent.

scenario: Sells drugs to high school sudents
moral_expl: Selling drugs to high school students exploits vulnerable youth, endangers health, and violates legal and ethical norms.

scenario: When my first husband and I split up, I was seventeen years old with a six-month-old baby and working in a grocery store. Sometimes I would just take things I needed. I remember taking cans of tuna, milk and loaves of bread.
moral_expl: Stealing is generally wrong, but context of youth, poverty, and providing for an infant reduces moral condemnation; survival needs temper the ethical judgment.

scenario: Person X was unable to fix the dripping faucet so they had to call the plumber.
moral_expl: Unable to fix a faucet and calling a professional is a reasonable and responsible action, reflecting practicality and awareness of one's limitations.

scenario: My friends and I were going to the movies and I was taking this guy with me in my car. He was being a jerk about my driving so I decided to go really fast and drive recklessly. It got to the point where he told me to stop the car so he could get out.
moral_expl: Reckless driving in response to criticism endangers lives and shows disregard for safety, making it morally unacceptable despite possible emotional provocation.

scenario: You see a mother forcing her daughter to enroll as a pre-med student in college.
moral_expl: Forcing someone into a career path disregards autonomy and personal choice, which is ethically problematic, though motivation may stem from care or concern.

scenario: Smiled and greeted people while walking down the street
moral_expl: Smiling and greeting others promotes friendliness, connection, and positive social interaction, though it's a minor moral act.

scenario: Was arrested on suspicion of child beating
moral_expl: Being arrested for suspected child beating implies serious harm to a vulnerable individual, which is widely regarded as deeply immoral; however, 'suspicion' means guilt is not confirmed, so score reflects alleged rather than proven behavior.

scenario: You see a woman using a fork to eat a bowl of vanilla ice cream and marshmallows.
moral_expl: Using a fork to eat ice cream and marshmallows is a matter of personal preference and has no significant moral implications.

scenario: You are on board a ship which is sinking. You are heading for the deck where the lifeboats are together with seven other people. You and another person have just crossed through a watertight door when it starts to close quickly. The other six people are behind the door, too far away to be able to pass through it in time.You knock the person next to you unconscious and use his body to block the door open. You know that this person will be crushed to death, but the other six will have time to save themselves.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save six involves severe harm, but the extreme life-or-death context and greater number saved create moral tension between duty to preserve life and minimizing overall harm.

scenario: Gave his seat to someone on the crowded bus
moral_expl: Giving up a seat on a crowded bus is a courteous and considerate act, especially if the recipient is more in need, reflecting kindness and social responsibility.

scenario: Told the owner of a store that she gave him too much change
moral_expl: Returning excess change demonstrates honesty, integrity, and respect for fairness, even when there is no obligation to do so.

scenario: I used to work for the federal government. I used a calculator from work at home for some audits. When I left government services, I kept the calculator.
