moral_expl: Keeping government property after leaving employment is a breach of trust and accountability, though the item's low value and personal use may mitigate severity.

scenario: You see a judge taking on a criminal case although he is friends with the defendant.
moral_expl: A judge presiding over a case involving a friend creates a conflict of interest, undermining impartiality, fairness, and public trust in the judicial system.

scenario: Translated menu items for a foreigner in a restaurant
moral_expl: Helping a foreigner understand the menu promotes kindness, inclusion, and effective communication in a social setting.

scenario: Person X bribed a landlord to be the first to get their apartment repainted.
moral_expl: Bribing a landlord for preferential treatment is unfair to others waiting for repairs and undermines equitable access to services, though harm may be relatively low in severity.

scenario: You are in charge of the division of a factory where wood panels are made. Because of a malfunction in one of the main machines, three workers have been caught up in a roller which is pulling them quickly into a very large press. The emergency system which blocks the machine has not activated and time is running out.You push the worker next to you into the cogs of the machine which operates the press to block the system. You know that this worker will die crushed, but the other three workers will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one worker to save three involves intentional harm, raising serious ethical concerns about using a person as a means to an end, despite the net lives saved.

scenario: You have taken your DVD player to be repaired at the shopping centre where you bought it. The technician told you it would cost €100 to repair. Some days later, passing by an electronics shop, you see a new and slightly better DVD reader in the window on sale for €120.You think for a bit and decide to buy the new DVD player, knowing well that the other one has already been repaired. You did not leave any contact details, and you will not go there again.
moral_expl: Deciding not to retrieve a repaired item is not inherently immoral, but failing to communicate or pay for services rendered violates fairness and诚信 (honesty). The technician performed work expecting compensation, and avoiding payment is ethically problematic.

scenario: Allowed an evicted friend to stay with him temporarily
moral_expl: Helping a friend in need by offering temporary shelter is generally seen as compassionate and supportive, reflecting empathy and loyalty.

scenario: One summer I had a job on the beach picking up trash. I found a wallet on the beach and it had $60 in it. So I picked it up and turned it in to my supervisor.
moral_expl: Returning a lost wallet with money shows honesty and integrity, especially in a role involving public trust. While not going above and beyond (e.g., attempting to locate the owner directly), turning it in officially is ethically commendable.

scenario: You see a man turn his back and walk away while his boss questions his work.
moral_expl: Walking away during a work discussion shows disrespect and avoidance, undermining professional communication and responsibility.

scenario: I was on vacation and rented a car. I paid for the parking fee and the guy gave me the wrong change back. I knew he gave me too much but I kept it anyway.
moral_expl: Keeping excess change is dishonest and exploits another's mistake, though harm is minor; it reflects poorly on integrity.

scenario: Person X closed the elevator door before an elderly neighbor could get in.
moral_expl: Closing the elevator door on an elderly neighbor shows disregard for courtesy, respect, and care for vulnerable individuals, though harm may be minor.

scenario: I lent my friend a large amount of money to get his car out of impound. He said he would pay me back, but then his wife showed up after several years. She took him for all his money, even the money he set aside for me, and he was embarrassed to tell me.
moral_expl: Lending money to help a friend in need is morally positive, but the situation involves broken promises and unfortunate circumstances beyond direct control. The friend's inability to repay due to unforeseen events reduces blame, though responsibility to communicate remains. The scenario reflects misfortune rather than clear moral wrongdoing.

scenario: Person X told a coworker that PersonX's brother was HIV-positive, a secret that their brother had told them in confidence.
moral_expl: Disclosing a deeply personal and sensitive health status without consent violates trust, confidentiality, and ethical boundaries, causing potential harm and stigma.

scenario: I took my sister's ring that was given to her by our father's aunt. I had never met our fatherÍs aunt but my sister spent some time with her. I took the ring because I didn't think that my sister deserved it.
moral_expl: Taking someone else's personal and sentimental property without consent is a violation of trust and ownership, even if motivated by personal judgment about worthiness.

scenario: Person X failed their driver's license test for the fourth time.
moral_expl: Failing a driver's license test multiple times is not a moral issue per se, but reflects lack of skill or preparation; it's neutral in moral terms as it involves no direct harm or ethical violation.

scenario: Stole Halloween candy
moral_expl: Stealing Halloween candy harms children's enjoyment and violates trust, though the stakes are relatively low compared to more serious theft.

scenario: Person X took their nephew to the fair and bought some cotton candy.
moral_expl: Taking a nephew to the fair and buying him cotton candy is a kind and nurturing act, reflecting positive familial engagement and generosity, though minor in moral significance.

scenario: One night I was going out late and I didn't want my son to know about it. I was a single mom and at that point in time he always seemed to want to act like the parent. So I snuck out of the house to go out.
moral_expl: Sneaking out to maintain personal freedom as a parent is understandable, but may undermine trust and transparency with a child.

scenario: Person X helped paint their neighbor's house even though it was PersonX's birthday.
moral_expl: Helping a neighbor despite a personal occasion shows generosity and selflessness, reflecting strong moral character.

scenario: A few years ago I was living alone. I had an enormous stereo system in my apartment and I used to turn it up very loud. One night I was drunk and turned it up to its maximum volume.
moral_expl: Playing loud music, especially while drunk, shows disregard for neighbors' peace and well-being, though not malicious, it reflects inconsiderate behavior.

scenario: Someone makes a post on Facebook about how people with disabilities are a drain on society
moral_expl: Publicly devaluing people with disabilities promotes discrimination, stigma, and harm, violating principles of dignity, equality, and compassion.

scenario: Shoved a man who was passing out leaflets
moral_expl: Physically assaulting someone for distributing leaflets violates personal autonomy and non-violence principles, even if the message is unpopular.

scenario: Circulated a petition in support of equal pay for women
moral_expl: Advocating for equal pay supports fairness, justice, and gender equality, which are widely regarded as moral imperatives.

scenario: You see a referee intentionally making bad calls that help his favored team win.
moral_expl: Intentionally making bad calls violates fairness, integrity, and the rules of sport, undermining trust and competitive equity.

scenario: Gave someone directions
moral_expl: Providing directions is a small act of kindness that helps others without significant cost, reflecting basic courtesy and social cooperation.

scenario: Person X volunteers to teach English to newly arrived immigrants.
moral_expl: Volunteering to teach English to immigrants supports integration, empowerment, and social inclusion, reflecting compassion and civic responsibility.

scenario: You see a woman clearly avoiding sitting next to an obese woman on the bus.
moral_expl: Avoiding someone based on appearance, such as body size, reflects prejudice and lack of empathy, contributing to social stigma and discrimination.

scenario: Person X found a wallet containing $1000 and returned it to its rightful owner.
moral_expl: Returning a lost wallet with its contents demonstrates honesty, integrity, and respect for others' property, especially given the significant amount of money involved.

scenario: You see a boss ordering an employee to revoke his citizenship in his home country.
moral_expl: No individual has the authority to demand someone else revoke their citizenship; such coercion violates personal autonomy, national identity, and basic human rights.

scenario: I have cheated pretty much all of my life. The most recent time was because I was in a long distance relationship. There were so many opportunities to cheat that I just gave into the temptation.
moral_expl: Repeated cheating, especially in relationships, violates trust, honesty, and commitment, which are foundational to healthy relationships. While temptation and emotional complexity may be present, habitual dishonesty reflects a significant moral failing.

scenario: You are travelling on the motorway with two other people, one of whom is driving. You are in the right-hand lane. Suddenly, the driver collapses onto the steering wheel and the car starts to gather speed. The truck in front of you brakes and the impact is unavoidable. To your right you notice a lay-by where there is a road worker.You swerve immediately over to the lay-by to avoid the truck. You know that the worker will be hit and killed, but you and the other two people in the car will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Swerving to save multiple lives at the cost of one is a tragic but often seen as morally justifiable under extreme duress, though it involves taking an active role in someone's death.

scenario: Tried to involve a shy friend in social activities
moral_expl: Encouraging a shy friend to participate in social activities shows care and support, though sensitivity to their comfort is important.

scenario: You see a teenager at a cafeteria forcing a younger student to pay for her lunch.
moral_expl: Bullying and coercing a younger student is a violation of fairness, consent, and basic respect, causing emotional and possibly physical harm.

scenario: Turned in someone else's project under his own name
moral_expl: Submitting someone else's work as your own is a clear violation of academic and ethical integrity, constituting plagiarism and deception.

scenario: Offers food and water to a wounded enemy combatant.
moral_expl: Offering food and water to a wounded enemy combatant reflects compassion, adherence to humanitarian principles, and respect for human dignity, even in conflict. This aligns with international ethical standards such as the Geneva Conventions.

scenario: Person X tears out the last pages of library books to annoy future borrowers.
moral_expl: Deliberately damaging shared resources harms others' access to knowledge and violates trust in communal spaces.

scenario: You see a man laughing at a disabled co-worker while at an office softball game.
moral_expl: Mocking someone with a disability is disrespectful, dehumanizing, and fosters a hostile environment, violating principles of empathy and dignity.

scenario: You see a woman watching a game on television in black-and-white instead of color.
moral_expl: Watching a TV game in black-and-white is a neutral personal choice with no inherent moral implications regarding harm, fairness, or ethical conduct.

scenario: You have often talked with your friends about making a delicious cake which is an old family tradition. You have finally decided to give them a surprise and have invited them to come round after dinner. In the afternoon, you get down to work on the recipe and realise that you do not have any dark chocolate which is what gives this cake its very particular taste.You use milk chocolate which you already have, so you do not have to go out again. You know that your friends do not know the recipe and will not realise.
moral_expl: Substituting ingredients for convenience is minor and well-intentioned; no harm is done, and the act is motivated by care and effort to surprise friends. While not fully authentic, the deception is negligible and contextually forgivable.

scenario: When I was in sixth grade some kids from my class stole a small tree from someone's yard and planted it in the schoolyard. I went over to the tree and stomped on it.
moral_expl: Stomping on a stolen tree may express disapproval of theft, but destroying property—even ill-gotten—is excessive and not a constructive response. A better approach would have been reporting the act or seeking adult guidance.

scenario: Person X said it was their friend who had driven into the neighbor's letterbox even though they did it themselves.
moral_expl: Falsely blaming someone else for your own mistake is dishonest and unfair, harming trust and potentially causing unjust consequences for the friend.

scenario: Participated in an effort to clean up a city park
moral_expl: Cleaning up a public space benefits the community and environment, reflecting civic responsibility and altruism.

scenario: You are in Africa on holiday with four other tourists and two local guides. One of the jeeps has broken down and the guide has left with the other to get help. While waiting, you decide to cross to the other side of the river in a small boat which you have found on the banks. Halfway across, you catch sight of two large crocodiles. The first violent flick of a tail almost tips the boat over.You throw one of the tourists into the water so that the crocodiles attack him. You know that he will die mauled, but you and the other four will be able to escape.
moral_expl: Sacrificing another person's life to save oneself and others is a severe violation of moral and ethical principles, even in life-threatening situations. Deliberately causing someone's death, especially without consent, is widely regarded as deeply immoral.

scenario: When I was a teenager, I took my father’s car out with my friends. My parents were out of town for a couple of days. I took the car without their permission to go for a joyride.
moral_expl: Taking a car without permission violates trust and rules, though intent was not malicious; common adolescent misjudgment reduces severity.

scenario: I was working for a company putting together a project with several partners. At one point I found out that there was a partner that was providing a great deal of money by importing marijuana from Thailand. I didn't say anything to anyone.
moral_expl: Remaining silent about illegal and potentially harmful activity compromises ethical responsibility and enables wrongdoing, though context like fear or loyalty may slightly mitigate blame.

scenario: Smoked in a no-smoking section even though others complained
moral_expl: Disregarding rules and others' complaints shows disrespect for shared spaces and public health considerations.

scenario: Sat in the wrong seat at a game, knowing that it wasn't his
moral_expl: Sitting in the wrong seat, especially knowingly, shows inconsideration for others' rights and can cause inconvenience, though harm is relatively minor.

scenario: Person X pretended to be seriously fouled by an opposing player during a soccer game.
moral_expl: Pretending to be fouled is deceptive and violates the principles of fair play and sportsmanship, undermining the integrity of the game.

scenario: Stopped to let another car into the line of traffic
moral_expl: Allowing another car to merge is a courteous and cooperative act that promotes traffic flow and mutual respect among drivers.

scenario: I worked at a job where I spoke with customers over the phone. One of my customers was in need of help because he was stranded in Romania. I did whatever I could to connect this person to people who could help him.
moral_expl: Going above and beyond to assist a stranded customer demonstrates empathy, compassion, and professional integrity, especially in a time of need.

scenario: Siphoned gas from a car parked on the street
moral_expl: Siphoning gas is theft and violates property rights, causing harm and financial loss to others.

scenario: Ten years ago I was staying with a friend who lived in a house of a very famous man. There were many autographed books in the house. I stole one of the books, which was autographed by a very famous celebrity.
moral_expl: Stealing is generally considered morally wrong, especially from someone else's personal property, even if the item seems insignificant to the owner. The act violates trust and property rights, regardless of the fame associated with the item.

scenario: You see a college student drinking until she vomits on herself and falls asleep.
moral_expl: Excessive drinking leading to self-harm raises concerns about personal responsibility and well-being, though it does not directly harm others; moral judgment is tempered by autonomy and context.

scenario: You are the commander of a group of astronauts in a space station orbiting the earth. Because of a breakdown, you have discovered a serious loss of pressurisation which in a short time will lead to the oxygen supply running out in the control cabin, where you and five other astronauts are. The emergency system is broken down and cannot be repaired immediately.You activate the bulkheads manually to isolate the depressurisation to just one cabin. You know that there are two astronauts there, and they will suffocate from a lack of oxygen, but you and the other five will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing two to save five involves a tragic but arguably necessary decision under extreme conditions; utilitarian reasoning supports the action, though it involves loss of life.

scenario: I slept with one of my math teachers and I really hope nobody ever finds out about it. At the time he said he was separated from his wife, so I knew that he was married.
moral_expl: Engaging in a sexual relationship with a teacher, especially when there is a power imbalance and potential breach of professional ethics, is widely considered morally inappropriate. Even if the teacher claimed to be separated, the relationship violates trust and ethical boundaries, particularly if the student was underage or in a vulnerable position. Such actions can cause harm to all parties involved, including reputational damage, emotional consequences, and institutional violations.

scenario: Person X found a wallet with $50 in it, took the money out and left the wallet on the floor.
moral_expl: Taking money and abandoning the wallet is dishonest and disregards the owner's loss, though harm is relatively minor compared to more severe thefts.

scenario: Person X won the door prize at the town's community fair.
moral_expl: Winning a door prize is a neutral event—neither inherently moral nor immoral—since it involves fair participation and no harm or ethical dilemma.

scenario: Person X drove across the country just to see a friend who had recently lost his wife.
moral_expl: Driving across the country to support a grieving friend demonstrates deep empathy, loyalty, and compassion, making it a highly considerate and morally commendable act.

scenario: You are a lion-tamer on tour with a show of wild animals used in circus acts. During the show, you notice that one of the lions has suddenly become aggressive. You see that it is not responding to your commands, is baring its teeth and is about to launch into the front row audience. Near you there are two assistants.You push the two assistants towards the lion so that it will attack them. You know that they will die mauled, but the audience will have time to escape to safety.
moral_expl: Sacrificing innocent lives to save others is a severe violation of moral and ethical duties; using others as mere means to an end is widely condemned.

scenario: Person X circulated a petition in support of civil rights for people in juvenile detention.
moral_expl: Advocating for civil rights, especially for vulnerable populations like youth in detention, reflects justice, empathy, and social responsibility.

scenario: Volunteered his time for a campaign to help save the seals
moral_expl: Volunteering to protect animals reflects compassion and environmental responsibility, though some may debate the prioritization of animal welfare over human needs.

scenario: Hit a car and left the scene of the accident
moral_expl: Leaving the scene of an accident is irresponsible and illegal, showing disregard for the safety and rights of others involved.

scenario: Comforted a man whose wife had recently died
moral_expl: Offering comfort to someone who is grieving is a compassionate and empathetic act, reflecting care and emotional support during a difficult time.

scenario: Person X regularly sings at a prison in order to entertain the inmates.
moral_expl: Singing to entertain inmates shows compassion and promotes rehabilitation, contributing positively to prisoner well-being without harm.

scenario: You see a man lashing his pony with a whip for breaking loose from its pen.
moral_expl: Using physical punishment on an animal for escaping may be seen as excessive and cruel, especially if done in anger rather than for training or safety. It reflects poorly on empathy and proportionality in discipline.

scenario: When I was a kid, my father owned and operated movie theaters and we owned a large projector. One time when I was in grammar school, I decided to steal a film that I liked. So I stole the film and brought it home to watch.
moral_expl: Stealing property, even as a child and for personal enjoyment, violates ownership rights and ethical norms, though intent and age may slightly mitigate culpability.

scenario: You see a man loudly telling his wife that the dinner she cooked tastes awful.
moral_expl: Publicly humiliating a partner by harshly criticizing their efforts is disrespectful and undermines kindness and empathy in relationships.

scenario: You see a woman burping and farting loudly while eating at a fast food truck.
moral_expl: While bodily functions are natural, doing so loudly in public disregards social norms and basic courtesy, causing discomfort to others.

scenario: Someone makes a post on Twitter making fun of police ofcers
moral_expl: Criticism or satire of authority figures, including police, is protected speech and can serve accountability, but mocking may undermine respect and inflame tensions depending on context and intent.

scenario: Person X hosted a fundraising dinner to raise money for a local homeless shelter.
moral_expl: Hosting a fundraising dinner for a homeless shelter demonstrates compassion, community support, and active effort to help vulnerable people, making it a morally commendable act.

scenario: Person X sneezed loudly in an important meeting.
moral_expl: Sneezing is an involuntary bodily function; while loud sneezing in a formal setting may be seen as disruptive, it is generally excusable if done without intent to disturb. Etiquette suggests covering the sneeze and apologizing, but the act itself is not morally significant.

scenario: Person X translated the menu items for a foreigner in a restaurant.
moral_expl: Helping someone understand a menu promotes kindness, inclusion, and communication across language barriers.

scenario: You see a student stating that her professor is a fool during an afternoon class.
moral_expl: Publicly calling a professor a fool is disrespectful and undermines constructive dialogue, though freedom of expression is valued; context and intent matter.

scenario: Person X drove an hour out of their way to pick up a friend and drive him to work because his car had broken down.
moral_expl: Going out of one's way to help a friend in need demonstrates kindness, reliability, and strong moral character.

scenario: Helped a man in a wheelchair cross a busy intersection
moral_expl: Assisting someone with a disability in a potentially dangerous situation demonstrates compassion, kindness, and civic responsibility.

scenario: Volunteered to stay late to help a coworker
moral_expl: Volunteering to help a coworker demonstrates kindness, teamwork, and solidarity, contributing positively to workplace morale and relationships.

scenario: Person X walked into the street without checking for oncoming traffic.
moral_expl: Walking into the street without checking traffic shows negligence, endangering oneself and potentially others, though not malicious.

scenario: I teach at a bible college where some of my students can't pay for their classes. So I covered them and told them that they could pay me later. I lied to the school and said that the students had given me the money.
moral_expl: Helping students access education is commendable, but lying to the institution undermines trust and institutional integrity, reducing moral acceptability.

scenario: You own a shop which sells fireworks. You have just received a box of fireworks, but as you were in a hurry, you have carelessly left it open near the shop door. Five customers are waiting. Another customer enters the shop. Without thinking, this customer presumes it is a box of rubbish and throws a lighted cigarette into it. The box is about to explode.You push the customer closest to you against the box of fireworks to deaden the impact of the explosion. You know that he will die, but you and the other five customers will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing an innocent person to save others involves intentional harm, which is ethically problematic despite the utilitarian outcome of saving more lives.

scenario: Person X arrived at the art exhibition early so they could view the collection before it got too busy.
moral_expl: Arriving early to enjoy an art exhibition before crowds is a personal choice that does not harm others and respects shared access to cultural experiences.

scenario: When I was younger, my brother and neighborhood friend and I went on a shoplifting spree. We took a lot of clothing. Then we got caught and we never stole again.
moral_expl: Shoplifting is morally wrong as it involves theft and harms others, but the fact that it occurred during youth and was followed by accountability and long-term reform mitigates the moral judgment somewhat.

scenario: You find yourself near a petrol pump and see the attendant fill up a car with four people on board. Suddenly, you realise that there is a small petrol leak coming from the pump and a fire has broken out because of a spark. It is about to spread quickly in the direction of the car.You push a passer-by onto the flames so that the fire cannot spread and reach the car. You know that the passer-by will die, but the other five people will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing an innocent bystander by actively pushing them into danger violates their rights and autonomy, even if it saves more lives. This act is seen as morally impermissible by deontological standards, despite potential utilitarian benefits.

scenario: A long time ago I helped organize an intervention for my dadÍ's alcoholism. My grandmother and I funded all of the members of the family to assemble together in one location. When my dad walked into the room he began to cry because he was so touched by our action.
moral_expl: Organizing an intervention to help a loved one overcome addiction shows compassion, care, and proactive support, especially when personally funded to ensure family unity. The emotional impact on the father further underscores the positive intent and moral value of the act.

scenario: I used to lie a lot when I was a little girl. My mother left my father when I was a year old. So I used to say that I had a mother and a father, even though my mother and grandmother raised me.
moral_expl: Childhood lying to fit in or cope with family complexity is common and often stems from emotional need rather than malice, reducing moral blame.

scenario: I used to ride the bus everyday to work. One day I noticed a pregnant woman who did not have a seat. So I took her by the arm and helped her find a seat.
moral_expl: Helping a pregnant woman find a seat demonstrates care and consideration, though physically guiding her may raise minor concerns about consent or autonomy.

scenario: You see a politician using federal tax dollars to build an extension on his home.
moral_expl: Using public funds for personal gain is a serious abuse of power and violates ethical and legal standards of public trust.

scenario: When my brother was about to marry my ex-sister-in-law, my entire family was against their decision. I approached my brother and asked him if he was sure he wanted to marry this girl. He got angry with me and said of course he did.
moral_expl: Expressing concern about a family member's life decision comes from care and love, even if poorly received; open communication in families is generally positive, though timing and approach matter.

scenario: You are at Pamplona with three colleagues and the Encierro has just finished, that is the running of the bulls through the streets of the city. While you are leaving, you realise that a bull has escaped from the corral and is heading towards you, attracted by your colleague’s red bag. You have your shoulders to a wall and there is no time to escape.You throw the red bag in the opposite direction. You know it will land near another person and that the bull will head for him/her, killing the person, but the four of you will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing an innocent person to save oneself and others is highly controversial; while done under duress, it involves actively redirecting harm to an uninvolved individual, violating moral responsibility not to cause harm.

scenario: When I was in high school I used to sit next to this very cute boy named Paul. We were taking a history test and I just wasn't prepared for it. So I looked at a couple of Paul's answers.
moral_expl: Cheating on a test violates academic integrity and fairness, even if the act seems minor or common; it undermines personal responsibility and trust.

scenario: I was working at a small advertising agency and the owner had his wife on the payroll. She rarely worked and I barely saw her. So, I was curious how much she was getting paid and looked through the record book.
moral_expl: Intruding on private financial records violates trust and confidentiality, even if motivated by curiosity or concern about unfairness.

scenario: Continually berated his wife in public
moral_expl: Publicly berating a spouse is emotionally abusive, disrespectful, and undermines dignity and healthy relationships.

scenario: Person X regularly steals office supplies from their job because they feel they deserve it.
moral_expl: Stealing, even small items, violates trust and company policy; feeling entitled does not justify unethical behavior.

scenario: Stole money and jewelry from the relatives he was living with
moral_expl: Stealing from family or those who provide shelter violates trust, loyalty, and basic ethical principles, making it highly immoral.

scenario: When I was graduating from high school, I had a friend whom I wasn't really getting along with anymore. He was coming to see me when he got into a car accident. I chose that time at the hospital to tell him our friendship was over.
moral_expl: Ending a friendship is a personal choice, but doing so while a friend is recovering from a traumatic event shows poor timing and lack of empathy.

scenario: I cheated on my present boyfriend. I didn't understand why he did not want to get married. I felt like he didn’t want to get married because of me and because he didn’t want to be with me, so I thought it was okay to have an affair.
moral_expl: Cheating violates trust and commitment in a relationship; rationalizing it based on personal insecurities does not justify the breach of fidelity.

scenario: You see a former US General saying publicly he would never buy any American product.
moral_expl: A former general choosing not to buy American products is a personal economic decision that may reflect protest or principle, but does not inherently harm others; however, it may be seen as disloyal given their past role, affecting public trust and national symbolism.

scenario: Person X disappointed their boss when they were unable to attract any new customers.
moral_expl: Failing to meet performance expectations is not inherently immoral; it's a professional setback rather than a moral failing.

scenario: Provided books to a nursing home
moral_expl: Providing books to a nursing home promotes enrichment, comfort, and mental stimulation for residents, reflecting kindness and social responsibility.

scenario: Person X brought in the groceries from the car and dropped one of the bags, which caused the eggs to break.
moral_expl: Accidentally dropping a grocery bag and breaking eggs is an unintentional mistake with no malicious intent, so it is generally not considered morally blameworthy.

scenario: Captures instead of kills a child soldier.
moral_expl: Capturing rather than killing a child soldier reflects restraint, recognition of the child's victimhood, and adherence to humanitarian principles, which is ethically commendable despite combat circumstances.

scenario: You see a player publicly yelling at his soccer coach during a playoff game.
moral_expl: Publicly yelling at a coach during a game shows disrespect, poor sportsmanship, and undermines team dynamics, though emotions can run high in competitive settings.

scenario: I left my second marriage and I left my step-kids there too. My youngest stepson has some disabilities, but I left him there. I could not cope with his druggy, drinking father and so I decided to leave everything behind.
moral_expl: Leaving a marriage due to an unhealthy environment is understandable, but abandoning a vulnerable child with special needs raises serious ethical concerns about responsibility and care.

scenario: I was in a relationship with this woman named Beth for a while. Then one night I cheated on Beth with my ex-girlfriend. The next morning my ex came by to see why I didn't stay the night at her place and she saw me with Beth.
moral_expl: Cheating violates trust and commitment in a relationship, causing emotional harm and demonstrating disrespect to one's partner.

scenario: You are a financial adviser who works for a big company. Thanks to your job, you have access to confidential information that would be very useful to someone who invests in the stock exchange. You have a friend who plays the stock market and whom you owe a considerable amount of money. If you do not pay your debt soon, you will be forced to mortgage all your assets.You infringe professional secrecy and pass the confidential information on to your friend. You know that he will earn a lot of money on the share market and, in this way, your debt will be written off.
moral_expl: Violating professional secrecy for personal gain undermines trust, breaks ethical and legal duties, and prioritizes self-interest over integrity and fairness.

scenario: Person X can walk on their hands down a flight of stairs.
moral_expl: Walking on hands down stairs is a physical skill with no inherent moral implications; it neither harms nor benefits others directly.

scenario: When I was little I would steal from supermarkets. I would steal things that my brothers and I wanted. I got caught a couple of times, but just kept doing it.
moral_expl: Stealing is ethically wrong and harms businesses, but the context of childhood and possible socioeconomic factors may mitigate moral blame.

scenario: Helped push someone's car out of a snow bank
moral_expl: Helping someone in need without expecting anything in return is a kind and socially responsible act, reflecting empathy and community spirit.

scenario: Person X accidentally knocked a glass off the table, but managed to catch it before it could smash on the floor.
moral_expl: Accidentally knocking over a glass is neutral, but catching it demonstrates attentiveness and prevents harm or mess, reflecting minor positive responsibility.

scenario: Helped pick up the papers dropped by another
moral_expl: Helping someone by picking up dropped papers is a small act of kindness that shows consideration and respect for others.

scenario: I was tempted not to tell the bank that they had made a $1200 error in my favor. I wanted to withdraw the money but at the same time I didn't want to go to jail. But I ended up calling the bank and told them the error.
moral_expl: Choosing to report a financial error despite temptation demonstrates honesty, integrity, and respect for fairness, even when personal gain was possible.

scenario: Put money in the meter for an unknown driver
moral_expl: Helping a stranger by preventing a parking violation is a kind and generous act, though minor in impact.

scenario: Refused to attend the funeral of a relative he disliked
moral_expl: Refusing to attend a funeral due to personal dislike is understandable but may be seen as disrespectful or hurtful to other family members, balancing personal boundaries and social expectations.

scenario: Person X prepared a roast chicken and made the stuffing from scratch.
moral_expl: Preparing a meal from scratch is a neutral to positive act, reflecting care and effort, but has no significant moral implications unless context involves harm or generosity.

scenario: Person X invited an unpopular coworker to have lunch with them at a new cafÃ© that had just opened.
moral_expl: Inviting an unpopular coworker to lunch promotes inclusion, kindness, and workplace positivity, reflecting empathy and moral consideration.

scenario: Ignored a new person in the office for several weeks
moral_expl: Ignoring a new colleague fosters exclusion and a hostile work environment, showing lack of kindness and professionalism.

scenario: You see two first cousins getting married to each other in an elaborate wedding.
moral_expl: Cousin marriage is culturally and legally variable; while some see it as a personal choice, others raise concerns about genetic risks and social norms, leading to mixed moral evaluations.

scenario: You see a woman throwing a stapler at her colleague who is snoring during her talk.
moral_expl: Throwing an object at someone is aggressive and potentially dangerous, even if provoked; it escalates conflict and violates norms of workplace conduct.

scenario: You see a student getting an A on a group project when he didn't do his part.
moral_expl: Receiving credit without contributing is unfair to others who did the work, undermining teamwork and academic integrity.

scenario: You see a student copying a classmate's answer sheet on a makeup final exam.
moral_expl: Cheating on an exam violates academic integrity, undermines fairness, and devalues honest effort, even if it's a makeup exam.

scenario: Started a false rumor about someone
moral_expl: Spreading false rumors harms reputations, erodes trust, and can cause emotional or social damage, making it ethically wrong in most contexts.

scenario: My mother has been ill for quite some time and I have been taking care of her and making sure she makes it to all her appointments. My mother made an appointment for the acupuncturist but didn't want to go. I got angry with her and made her go.
moral_expl: Forcing someone to attend medical care out of concern may come from a place of love and responsibility, but it risks overriding personal autonomy, especially if the person is mentally competent. The intention is caring, yet the method is coercive, which reduces moral acceptability.

scenario: You see a teacher publicly saying she hopes another school wins the math contest.
moral_expl: A teacher expressing support for a rival school may seem unprofessional or disloyal, but it does not cause direct harm and could reflect sportsmanship or a belief in fairness; context matters.

scenario: Person X did all the repair work on their car.
moral_expl: Performing personal maintenance on one's own property is a neutral act that shows responsibility and self-reliance, but has no significant moral implications for others.

scenario: Ridiculed a handicapped child by making fun of him
moral_expl: Mocking a vulnerable child with a disability is deeply cruel, harmful, and violates fundamental ethical principles of compassion and respect.

scenario: Refused to help a child fix his bicycle
moral_expl: Refusing to help a child may encourage self-reliance, but could also be seen as lacking compassion or mentorship, depending on context.

scenario: Person X likes to go to their local cafÃ© and sip coffee while reading the newspaper.
moral_expl: Enjoying coffee and reading the news is a neutral, personal activity that neither harms nor significantly benefits others.

scenario: Bought food for everyone in the office
moral_expl: Buying food for others is a generous and thoughtful act that fosters goodwill and community in the workplace.

scenario: You are carrying out research into the behaviour of lions in the Savannah of Central Africa. From an observation tower, you can see four people hide behind a bush. They are being followed by a lion and are trying to get away from it. The lion has seen them and is heading for the bush. Another person has been able to climb the observation tower.You push off the person who has climbed the tower so that the lion is drawn towards him. You know that this person will be mauled to death, but the other four will have time to escape.
moral_expl: Sacrificing an innocent person by pushing them to their death is a direct act of harm, which is widely seen as morally impermissible, even to save more lives. This violates ethical principles against using people as means to an end.

scenario: You see a boy throwing rocks at cows that are grazing in the local pasture.
moral_expl: Throwing rocks at animals causes unnecessary harm and distress, reflecting poor empathy and respect for living beings.

scenario: Person X was forced to pay multiple bank fees for not paying their credit card repayment on time.
moral_expl: Bank fees for late payments are a standard financial practice intended to encourage timely repayment, but high or repeated fees can be seen as exploitative, especially if the individual is in financial distress. The morality depends on the fairness of the fee structure and the bank's transparency.

scenario: You are the foreman of a construction company and are checking on the work of a team on the top floor of a skyscraper. At a certain point, you realise that a steel cable is about to give way. This cable supports the external platform where you are, and the five men working on a support structure.You throw one of the workers off the platform to lighten the load. You know that he will die in the fall, but you and the other four will have time to get off and to be safe.
moral_expl: Deliberately killing an innocent worker to save others is a severe violation of personal rights and ethical duty, even in life-threatening situations. Though the outcome saves more lives, the act of throwing someone to their death is widely seen as morally impermissible.

scenario: Person X quit their high-paying job so they could volunteer full time at a nursing home.
moral_expl: Quitting a high-paying job to volunteer full time at a nursing home reflects strong altruism and dedication to helping others, though personal and economic context may influence judgment. The act prioritizes care for vulnerable people over personal gain, which is widely seen as morally commendable.

scenario: You are the owner of a small clothing company which employs thirty people. Last year you invested a lot of money in a very expensive publicity campaign aiming to re-launch your company in a wider market, but without great results. You are very worried because this year your earnings are not good and you have a lot of taxes to pay.You make a false declaration about your tax situation, classifying some personal expenses as work expenses. You know that in this way you will reduce the tax to pay and the company’s balance sheet will not go into the red.
moral_expl: Falsifying tax declarations is legally and ethically wrong, even if done to protect jobs and business stability; it undermines fairness and trust in the tax system.

scenario: Person X put up posters and handed out fliers to help find their neighbor's missing dog.
moral_expl: Helping to find a neighbor's missing dog demonstrates kindness, community spirit, and empathy, making it a morally positive action.

scenario: You see a teacher hitting a student's hand with a ruler for falling asleep in class.
moral_expl: Physical punishment of students is widely viewed as abusive and ineffective, violating children's rights and dignity.

scenario: Used stolen food stamps
moral_expl: Using stolen food stamps is unethical as it involves theft and misuse of resources intended for those in need, undermining trust and support systems.

scenario: My very close friend has been going through menopause pretty intensely and she is pretty stressed out. She has been very hard to deal with, so I decided to talk to her about it. I was afraid she might blow up at me but she listened to me with an open heart.
moral_expl: Supporting a close friend during a difficult life stage with empathy and care demonstrates compassion and strengthens relationships, even if the conversation carries emotional risk.

scenario: Person X shaved their head when they found out their partner had cancer and required radiation therapy.
moral_expl: Shaving one's head to show solidarity and support for a partner undergoing cancer treatment demonstrates empathy, compassion, and emotional support, which are highly valued moral actions.

scenario: Someone disposes of a small bag of garbage by throwing it into a nearby forest
moral_expl: Littering harms the environment, pollutes natural spaces, and shows disregard for public welfare and ecological responsibility.

scenario: Hit a dog and drove away without notifying the owner
moral_expl: Hitting a dog and fleeing shows disregard for animal welfare, responsibility, and legal/ethical duties, making it highly immoral.

scenario: Person X forgot to turn the stove off before leaving the house.
moral_expl: Forgetting to turn off the stove poses a serious safety risk to self and others, potentially leading to fire or harm, though it stems from negligence rather than intent.

scenario: Loudly talked to a companion in a movie
moral_expl: Talking loudly during a movie disrupts others' experience and disregards shared social norms of courtesy and respect in public spaces.

scenario: Visited a sick friend in the hospital
moral_expl: Visiting a sick friend demonstrates care, empathy, and support, strengthening personal relationships and providing emotional comfort during hardship.

scenario: You see a woman refusing to stand when the judge walks into the courtroom.
moral_expl: Refusing to stand in court may be seen as disrespectful to legal authority and social norms, but could reflect a principled stand or protest, depending on context.

scenario: Loudly criticized the art at a community children's art show
moral_expl: Publicly criticizing children's art can be hurtful and discouraging, undermining their confidence and the supportive intent of community events.

scenario: Someone posts online about a private conversation they overheard containing what they consider ofensive humor, including a picture of those involved in the conversation
moral_expl: Sharing private conversations without consent violates privacy and can cause public shaming, even if the content is offensive; this undermines trust and proportionality in response.

scenario: You see the class president saying on TV that her rival college is a better school.
moral_expl: Speaking honestly, even when it's unfavorable to one's own group, demonstrates integrity and intellectual honesty, which are morally commendable traits.

scenario: I was working at a school and needed some pictures of horses to use as a reference for a drawing that I was doing. So I used some of the books that were at the school and have never returned them. I left the school some time ago.
moral_expl: Taking school property without permission, even for a personal project, is a breach of trust and responsibility, though intent was not malicious.

scenario: Someone who is a United States citizen burns an American fag at a protest
moral_expl: Burning a flag is a protected form of symbolic speech in the U.S., expressing dissent, but deeply offends many people's sense of patriotism and respect for national symbols.

scenario: Ridicules people behind their backs
moral_expl: Ridiculing others behind their backs is dishonest and hurtful, damaging trust and respect in relationships.

scenario: Someone creates an account on a website intended to help people commit adultery without their spouseâ€™s knowledge
moral_expl: Facilitating adultery undermines trust, honesty, and commitment in relationships, which are foundational to marital ethics, though some may view it as enabling personal autonomy.

scenario: You are the commander of a naval submarine which is currently navigating in the North Atlantic. The sonar reveals the presence of a torpedo which is heading at great speed towards a small boat with six people on board, which is offshore. Close by, there is also a naval patrol boat with two people on board.You launch false targets to change the trajectory of the torpedo. You know that it will hit the patrol boat killing the two people on board, but the six people in the boat will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Sacrificing two to save six involves a utilitarian calculation where more lives are preserved, but actively diverting harm raises ethical concerns about intention and responsibility.

scenario: Let a civic group hold weekly meetings in his home
moral_expl: Allowing a civic group to meet at home supports community engagement and democratic participation, reflecting generosity and civic responsibility.

scenario: Could not remember the birthdays of his family members
moral_expl: Forgetting birthdays is common and not inherently immoral, though it may reflect a lack of attentiveness or thoughtfulness in personal relationships.

scenario: You see a man telling a woman that her painting looks like it was done by children.
moral_expl: Critiquing someone's art harshly and disrespectfully can be hurtful and discouraging, though intent and context (e.g., constructive criticism vs. mockery) matter. As presented, the comment appears unkind and unnecessarily demeaning.

scenario: You see a very drunk woman making out with multiple strangers on the city bus.
moral_expl: The behavior involves impaired judgment and potential vulnerability, raising concerns about consent and safety; public intoxication and risky actions can lead to harm, though the person may not intend malice.

scenario: Pushes an enemy combatant off a one-story balcony.
moral_expl: Using physical force against an enemy combatant may be contextually justified in combat, but pushing from a balcony risks excessive harm; morality depends on proportionality and rules of engagement.

scenario: You see a teenage girl coming home late and ignoring her parents' strict curfew.
moral_expl: Breaking a curfew is a minor act of disobedience; context such as safety and parental rules affects judgment, but it does not involve severe harm or ethical violation.

scenario: You see a teenage boy chuckling at an amputee he passes by while on the subway.
moral_expl: Mocking someone with a disability is deeply disrespectful and cruel, showing a lack of empathy and basic human decency.

scenario: Refused to let the neighborhood children play in his yard
moral_expl: Property owners have the right to control access to their yard, though kindness and community spirit might encourage sharing; refusal is not inherently harmful but may seem ungenerous.

scenario: Defaced a large rock with spray paint in a public park
moral_expl: Defacing public property with spray paint is vandalism, showing disregard for community spaces and rules, even if intended as artistic expression.

scenario: You are eating a delicious meal that you have just prepared when the phone rings. It is the representative of an important survey company. He tells you that if you can spend half an hour of your time replying to a series of questions about the development of alternative energy, his company will send you a voucher for €80 to spend on books.You say you are interested, but ask if you can be called back later saying that you have a bad headache. You know that this means you can finish your meal and receive the voucher.
moral_expl: Deceiving the survey representative to delay participation for personal convenience is minor dishonesty with minimal harm, but still undermines truthfulness and fairness.

scenario: Back in high school I kind of had an agreement with the guy sitting next to me. We would show each other our papers whenever we were taking a test. Both of us were pretty good students, we just would make sure we shared test answers if we needed to.
moral_expl: Cheating on tests violates academic integrity and fairness, even if both parties agree; undermines learning and merit.

scenario: You are a building worker who is manoeuvring a crane on a building site. You have just started your day on the site, when you realise that the cable of the crane is about to break. Attached to the cable is an enormous steel beam which is directly above a crew of six who are working on the outside of a building in construction.You move the arm of the crane a short distance to another area of the site. You know that there is a worker there who will be crushed by the steel beam and will die, but the other six workers will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Diverting the crane to save six workers at the cost of one life involves a difficult moral trade-off, but many would see it as a tragic necessity to minimize overall harm.

scenario: Put sugar in his friend's gas tank
moral_expl: Sabotaging someone's property, even as a prank, can cause significant harm, expense, and danger, making it ethically unjustifiable.

scenario: You see a boy making fun of his brother for getting dumped by his girlfriend.
moral_expl: Mocking someone during emotional distress shows insensitivity and lack of empathy, especially among family.

scenario: Person X joined the military because they wanted to see what it was like to kill people.
moral_expl: Joining the military to experience killing reflects a dangerous and unethical motivation, as it glorifies violence and disregards the value of human life.

scenario: Instead of contributing more to my mother's economic stability, by helping her out monetarily in her retirement, I traveled. I spent money on travel, on vacations. Some of that money could've gone to her.
moral_expl: Prioritizing personal travel over supporting a parent in retirement may reflect self-interest, but responsibility to support parents varies by context and cultural expectations.

scenario: You see a woman lying about the number of vacation days she has taken at work.
moral_expl: Falsifying vacation days violates honesty and fairness, undermining workplace trust and accountability.

scenario: Person X loosened the wheel nuts of their neighbor's car, because the neighbor always played loud music.
moral_expl: Sabotaging someone's vehicle is dangerous and illegal, posing serious risk of harm or death, even if motivated by frustration over noise.

scenario: Person X left the family business to set up their own business, taking most of the clients with them and causing the family business to go bankrupt.
moral_expl: Leaving a family business is acceptable, but taking clients and causing bankruptcy shows disloyalty and lack of integrity, harming family trust and economic stability.

scenario: Person X did not attend their coworker's funeral because they'd had a disagreement before the coworker died.
moral_expl: Skipping a funeral due to a past disagreement shows a lack of compassion and closure, though personal emotions may complicate the decision.

scenario: You see a single man ordering an inflatable sex doll that looks like his secretary.
moral_expl: Personal choices about private purchases are generally seen as morally neutral, though some may view the objectification of others as ethically questionable; however, no direct harm is evident.

scenario: Helped a neighbor fix his roof
moral_expl: Helping a neighbor with a necessary repair is a kind, prosocial act that strengthens community and shows goodwill.

scenario: About 10 years ago I was riding my bike and passed a Ford truck with the keys in the door. So I went into the apartment building facing the street and asked the man inside if he had a ford truck. He did and he went outside to get his keys out of the car.
moral_expl: Acting to prevent potential theft or harm by helping an unaware owner secure their vehicle shows initiative and community-mindedness, though slightly intrusive in approach.

scenario: You see a woman continuing to wear a large sun hat inside her apartment complex.
moral_expl: Wearing a sun hat indoors is unusual but harmless; it does not affect others or violate moral norms, so it's largely a matter of personal choice.

scenario: Person X once spent a whole weekend watching furniture restoration videos on the Internet.
moral_expl: Watching furniture restoration videos is a neutral personal activity with no significant moral implications; it neither harms nor directly benefits others.

scenario: Person X worked in a factory and put broken glass in packets of children's cereal.
moral_expl: Deliberately endangering children's lives by tampering with food is highly dangerous, malicious, and a severe violation of ethical and legal standards.

scenario: Went to the grocery store with his kids
moral_expl: Going to the grocery store with children is a neutral, everyday activity that involves no clear moral violation or ethical action.

scenario: Person X laughed at a friend's joke even though it wasn't funny.
moral_expl: Laughing at a friend's joke insincerely is a minor social courtesy that can maintain harmony, though it involves slight dishonesty.

scenario: You are in charge of a work crew who are doing repair work for the railways. In the distance you see a trolley and realise that the driver has lost control of it. If the trolley continues on it will end up running into five workers who are working on the tracks. On a secondary track there is one worker.You pull a lever on the interchange which will divert the trolley onto the secondary track. You know that it will run into and kill the worker, but the other workers will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Diverting the trolley to save five at the cost of one life is a classic ethical dilemma; many view it as a utilitarian choice to minimize harm, though it involves active intervention leading to death.

scenario: Calls off an airstrike because he sees civilians present.
moral_expl: Choosing not to proceed with an airstrike upon seeing civilians demonstrates respect for human life and adherence to ethical and legal principles of warfare, particularly distinction and proportionality.

scenario: When I was about 20 years old I didn't really want to date just one guy. So I started dating four guys at one time. I snuck around and hung out with each one at different times, but didn't tell any of them that I was dating the others.
moral_expl: Deceiving multiple partners undermines honesty and trust, which are foundational in relationships; though personal freedom is valued, lack of transparency is ethically problematic.

scenario: You see a woman eating dessert before her main entree arrives on the table.
moral_expl: Eating dessert before the main course is a personal choice and social norm violation at most, not a moral issue. It does not harm others or violate ethical principles.

scenario: Person X didn't go to a concert they had been looking forward to because their mother was ill.
moral_expl: Prioritizing care for an ill family member over personal enjoyment demonstrates responsibility and compassion, which is generally seen as morally commendable.

scenario: During the Vietnam War, I received a religious opt-out and got out of going to Vietnam. I felt very guilty and as though I should be in the army fighting. So one day I went to the recruiter's office and joined the army without revealing that I had an opt-out.
moral_expl: Voluntarily joining the military despite having a legitimate exemption demonstrates moral responsibility and courage, especially when driven by a sense of duty and guilt over perceived unfair advantage. While deceptive in not disclosing the opt-out, the intent to serve and share the burden of war elevates the moral standing.

scenario: Disarms an enemy combatant without killing him.
moral_expl: Disarming an enemy without killing them shows restraint, reduces harm, and respects the value of human life, especially in conflict situations where lethal force may be expected but avoided.

scenario: You see a manager taking half the doughnuts from a box, leaving little for others.
moral_expl: Taking an unfair share of shared food shows inconsideration and greed, especially when it leaves little for others, undermining workplace fairness and courtesy.

scenario: While I was in a relationship I found another man very attractive. I didn't want to be unfaithful to my partner but I did flirt with this guy a couple of times. I gave him a ride home one day too.
moral_expl: Flirting with someone else while in a committed relationship breaches trust and fidelity, even if physical infidelity is avoided. Intentions matter, but emotional betrayal can still harm the relationship.

scenario: Intentionally wounds rather than kills a fleeing enemy combatant.
moral_expl: Intentionally wounding instead of killing a fleeing combatant shows restraint and adherence to ethical warfare principles, reducing unnecessary harm while neutralizing threat.

scenario: You see a girl telling her classmate that she looks like she has gained weight.
moral_expl: Making negative comments about someone's appearance is hurtful and disrespectful, damaging self-esteem and social trust.

scenario: You see a teacher ordering a student to get a normal haircut before coming to class.
moral_expl: Enforcing grooming standards may reflect institutional rules, but can also be seen as overly controlling or discriminatory depending on context, potentially disregarding personal expression and cultural differences.

scenario: Person X always wrote things down as they would always forget things.
moral_expl: Writing things down to remember is a neutral, practical behavior that shows responsibility and self-awareness, with no significant moral implications.

scenario: Makes a group of POWs strip naked and lay on top of each other.
moral_expl: This act involves severe dehumanization, humiliation, and physical coercion of vulnerable individuals, violating fundamental human rights and international laws regarding the treatment of prisoners of war.

scenario: You are a motorcyclist and you are taking part in a motocross race. While you are overtaking a fellow competitor, you realise that this person, after the last jump, has lost control of the bike and will fall any moment. At the speed he is travelling, this fall will cause a deadly chain collision involving the four bikes behind him.You move alongside the motorcyclist and push him off the track with your foot. You know that he will crash into the fence and die, but in this way the other four motorcyclists will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Intentionally causing someone's death, even to prevent greater harm, violates moral norms against killing and personal autonomy. While the outcome saves more lives, the act of deliberately causing a fatality is ethically troubling and generally seen as unacceptable in deontological frameworks.

scenario: Burns a local villageâ€™s crops after they donâ€™t cooperate.
moral_expl: Destroying crops as punishment harms innocent people, causes suffering, and violates principles of justice and proportionality.

scenario: You see someone using an old rotary phone and refusing to go buy a new one.
moral_expl: Using an old rotary phone is a personal choice and does not inherently harm others; it may reflect preference, nostalgia, or resistance to consumerism, but has minimal moral significance.

scenario: When I was getting married, my husband-to-be and I had to sit down with the minister and talk about our vows. I was reluctant to do this because of my past negative experiences with the church.  I had become a pagan.  But I discussed this openly and the minister embraced me.
moral_expl: Open dialogue about beliefs and mutual respect in a spiritual setting promotes understanding and inclusivity, which is ethically positive.

scenario: You see a man having sex with a frozen chicken before cooking it for dinner.
moral_expl: While the act is unusual and may be seen as bizarre or distasteful, it does not involve harm to sentient beings or clear violation of rights; moral judgment is limited due to lack of harm or consent issues with non-sentient objects.

scenario: You see a mayor trying to ban people from hugging and kissing in public in his city.
moral_expl: Banning public affection infringes on personal freedoms and emotional expression, generally seen as an overreach of authority unless justified by extreme circumstances, which are not indicated here.

scenario: You see a mother telling her son that she is going to choose all of his friends.
moral_expl: While parental guidance is expected, dictating a child's friendships undermines their autonomy and social development, making it overly controlling.

scenario: Insulted a stranger by making a racial slur
moral_expl: Using a racial slur is deeply offensive, promotes hate, and dehumanizes others, violating principles of respect and dignity.

scenario: Ignored a woman's cry for help in lifting a baby carriage
moral_expl: Ignoring someone in need, especially when assistance is simple and harmless to provide, reflects indifference and lack of compassion, though not actively harmful.

scenario: Person X went to purchase a new pair of shoes but couldn't find any that were comfortable.
moral_expl: The scenario involves no moral action or consequence; it's a neutral personal experience without ethical implications for others.

scenario: Person X forgave their partner even though they had been cheating on PersonX for two years.
moral_expl: Forgiveness after betrayal can be a morally commendable act of empathy and compassion, though it may depend on context such as accountability and personal boundaries. It reflects emotional maturity and a desire for reconciliation, but could be seen as enabling if done without change. Overall, it leans positive but is complex.

scenario: You see a woman drinking her entire cup of coffee using a stirring spoon.
moral_expl: Drinking coffee with a spoon is unusual and inefficient, but it causes no harm to others and is largely a matter of personal quirk or choice. It may seem odd, but it does not violate moral norms.

scenario: You see a man wearing clothes that are clearly several sizes too big for his body.
moral_expl: Wearing ill-fitting clothes is a personal choice or circumstance and does not inherently involve moral harm to others.

scenario: You see a girl laughing when she realizes her friend's dad is the janitor.
moral_expl: Laughing at someone's social status or occupation shows disrespect and reinforces class-based prejudice, which is ethically problematic.

scenario: Someone posts a picture of a person on Twitter without their knowledge, making fun of their weighte
moral_expl: Posting someone's image online without consent to mock them is a violation of privacy and dignity, causing emotional harm and promoting bullying.

scenario: You see a man leaving his family business to go work for their main competitor.
moral_expl: Leaving a family business for a competitor is a personal career choice, but may be seen as disloyal; however, individuals have autonomy over their professional paths.

scenario: When I was 17 years old I had two boyfriends. I would see one and then go visit the other. Sometimes one would drive up to my house as the other was driving away. Neither of them ever found out.
moral_expl: Maintaining two romantic relationships simultaneously without honesty or disclosure violates trust and ethical standards in relationships, though no physical harm is done.

scenario: You are passing over the Swiss Alps in a hot air balloon together with four other people. You have just reached an altitude of 2,000 metres. At a certain point you become aware of a malfunction which is causing you to lose altitude faster and faster. You are moving towards a very big mountain chain and if you cannot gain altitude quickly, the balloon will crash into the rock face.To lighten the load and cause the balloon to ascend, you throw one of the people overboard. You know that he will die, but you and the other three will survive.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one person to save others involves intentional killing, which is ethically grave, but the extreme life-or-death context and survival of the majority may lend some utilitarian justification. However, actively throwing someone to their death violates strong moral prohibitions against killing and consent, making it highly controversial.

scenario: Person X was in a car accident but drove off before they could see if anyone was injured.
moral_expl: Leaving the scene of an accident without checking for injuries is irresponsible and potentially illegal, showing disregard for others' well-being.

scenario: Started an argument with a coworker
moral_expl: Starting an argument with a coworker is generally unproductive and can harm workplace harmony, though context may affect severity.

scenario: You are a member of the Air Force and the commander of a squad of planes that is on a reconnaissance mission. On one of these missions, while flying over an inhabited area, you realise that one of the planes has just launched a missile accidentally, and it is heading directly for a house in which a family of three lives.You hit the missile to divert it. You know that it will fall on a street in which there is a car with a person in it. This person will die, but the family of three will be saved.
moral_expl: Diverting the missile to save three lives at the cost of one involves a difficult moral trade-off, but aligns with utilitarian principles of minimizing harm. The action is taken under duress and with intent to reduce loss of life, though it still involves intentional redirection toward an innocent person.

scenario: Person X went to the nearby airport, as they like to watch the planes.
moral_expl: Going to an airport to watch planes is a harmless and neutral activity that poses no ethical concerns.

scenario: Went out of his way to help a foreigner find the bus station
moral_expl: Helping a foreigner demonstrates kindness, empathy, and civic virtue, especially when it involves personal effort.

scenario: You are at the supermarket and you have just finished doing the shopping. You go to the checkout queue. When it comes to your turn, you immediately realise that the cashier, distracted by a conversation with a colleague, has given you €30 too much change.You immediately put the money in your pocket to avoid the cashier realising. You know that she will probably have to reimburse the till for the missing money from her own pocket, but it was her fault since she was distracted.
moral_expl: Keeping excess change despite knowing the cashier will suffer financial consequences is dishonest and exploits another's mistake, reflecting poorly on integrity and fairness.

scenario: Person X helped their brother renovate his house every night for six months after it had been damaged by fire.
moral_expl: Helping a family member recover from a disaster through sustained effort demonstrates loyalty, compassion, and strong moral character.

scenario: Filed a complaint against some teenagers for trespassing
moral_expl: Filing a complaint against trespassing is a reasonable action to protect property and safety, though context like intent or harm level may influence judgment.

scenario: You are walking along the road when you see a wallet on the ground. You open it and see that it contains several hundred euros and the owner’s licence. From the credit card and other evidence you realise that the owner is very rich. You are going through a difficult time and really need the money.You remove the cash and put the wallet in the post. In this way you can pay some of your bills and the legitimate owner will have his documents and credit card back.
moral_expl: Taking money from a wallet, even if returning documents, violates trust and property rights, though returning ID and cards mitigates harm slightly.

scenario: I was thirteen years old and I went into the grocery store where I lived. There was a comb that I wanted in the store, so I just took it. I didn't really need it but I just wanted the thrill of stealing it and nobody catching me.
moral_expl: Stealing, even as a minor and for thrill-seeking, violates ethical norms of honesty and property rights, though immaturity may slightly mitigate culpability.

scenario: Attempted to rape a woman who was walking down a dark street
moral_expl: Attempted rape is a severe violation of autonomy, dignity, and bodily integrity, constituting a grave moral and criminal wrong.

scenario: You see a boy skipping to the front of the line because his friend is an employee.
moral_expl: Skipping the line using personal connections violates fairness and undermines equal treatment for others waiting.

scenario: Person X received the employee of the month award at their work.
moral_expl: Receiving an employee of the month award is generally positive, reflecting recognition for hard work and contribution, though the moral implications depend on how the award was earned. Assuming fair and honest performance, it reflects commendable professional behavior.

scenario: My wife was diagnosed with cancer. I was there with her every step of the way even though it was extremely emotionally and mentally demanding. I helped her through all her appointments and emotional distress.
moral_expl: Supporting a spouse through illness with compassion and commitment reflects strong moral character, empathy, and dedication.

scenario: I found out that my friend had said some mean things behind my back. I wanted to tell my other friend that this mutual friend of ours had said some mean things about me behind my back. I still haven't decided if I should tell her or not.
moral_expl: Sharing negative information about someone can damage relationships and spread negativity, but may be justified if done to warn or protect others; however, doing so out of retaliation or gossip lowers moral acceptability. The indecision reflects moral complexity, but the act itself risks harm and breaches of trust.

scenario: Someone throws a kitten as part of a joke, causing it to yelp on impact
moral_expl: Throwing a kitten for amusement causes harm and distress to an innocent animal, reflecting cruelty and disregard for sentient life.

scenario: You see a wife hitting her husband on the side of his head for coming home late.
moral_expl: Physical violence in a relationship is generally considered morally wrong, regardless of the target, as it undermines safety, respect, and healthy conflict resolution.

scenario: You see a tenant bribing a landlord to be the first to get their apartment repainted.
moral_expl: Bribery undermines fairness and equal treatment, giving unfair advantage and eroding trust in systems.

scenario: Person X saw a child lost in a supermarket, so they helped find the parents by alerting the staff.
moral_expl: Helping a lost child by alerting staff is a responsible and compassionate act that ensures the child's safety and well-being.

scenario: Helped a lost child find her parents in a store
moral_expl: Helping a lost child is a compassionate and responsible act that ensures safety and alleviates distress for the child and family.

scenario: Person X forgot it was their wedding anniversary and did not have a gift for their spouse.
moral_expl: Forgetting an anniversary reflects thoughtlessness and lack of consideration, but it is a common human error without malicious intent.

scenario: You are a secret service agent on a mission overseas. You have discovered that a terrorist group is attempting to assassinate three diplomats. They are travelling in a car towards the embassy. They are being followed by one of the terrorists who will take advantage of the first red traffic light to kill them. As you follow, a tanker carrying oil slips between your car and the terrorist’s.You shoot at the tanker, which will injure the terrorist when it explodes so that he will not be able to complete his mission. You know that the explosion will kill the tanker driver, but the three diplomats will not be injured.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one innocent life (the tanker driver) to prevent a greater harm (assassination of three diplomats) involves a difficult moral trade-off. While the outcome saves more lives, intentionally causing the death of an uninvolved party violates ethical principles like non-combatant immunity and proportionality. However, the intent is to prevent imminent violence, which increases moral justification compared to reckless or selfish acts.

scenario: You see a woman swerving her car in order to intentionally run over a squirrel.
moral_expl: Intentionally harming an animal out of malice or indifference is ethically wrong and reflects poor moral judgment.

scenario: Shot a songbird with his .22 caliber rifle
moral_expl: Shooting a songbird with a rifle is seen as cruel and unnecessary, especially if done without purpose like food or conservation; it shows disregard for animal life.

scenario: Laughed at a person who tripped on a curb and fell
moral_expl: Laughing at someone's misfortune shows a lack of empathy and kindness, though it may sometimes occur as an involuntary reaction; it is generally considered socially and morally insensitive.

scenario: You are the pilot of a fighter jet on a mission in South East Asia. During the flight you realize that another fighter jet on the same mission has launched a missile by mistake and it is aimed at a six-seater light aircraft. You notice that in the area of the missile there is also another military reconnaissance aircraft.Start the procedure to destroy the missile. You know that when it explodes it will cause the military reconnaissance aircraft to crash and the pilot will be killed, but the six people aboard the light aircraft will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one trained military pilot to save six civilians aligns with utilitarian principles of minimizing loss of life, though intentionally causing a death raises ethical concerns.

scenario: Shared his umbrella with someone during the rain
moral_expl: Sharing an umbrella in the rain is a kind and considerate act that demonstrates empathy and willingness to help others at minimal personal cost.
